Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
We power people’s progress.

At Preply, we’re all about creating life-changing learning experiences. We help people discover the magic of the perfect tutor, craft a personalised learning journey, and stay motivated to keep growing. Our approach is human-led, tech-enabled - and it’s creating real impact.

We’ve just reached unicorn status with a $150M Series D, accelerating our vision to transform education through human-led, AI-enhanced learning. Today, 100,000+ tutors teach 90+ languages to learners in 180 countries - and we’re only getting started. As a category-defining company, we’re shaping what the future of learning looks like at global scale.

Every Preply lesson sparks change, fuels ambition, and drives progress that matters. Joining Preply means helping define the future of education at global scale, and building something that truly matters for millions of people, every day.

Meet the team!

At Preply, data is at the heart of every decision we make. We run hundreds of analysis to continually optimize our product, each with its own analytical and tracking challenges. The complexity of our subscription model, along with the unique dynamics of tutor-learner interactions, offers an exciting opportunity for those looking to make a real impact.

As a Staff Data Analyst , you will own the data strategy for a critical product domain (e.g., Growth, Retention, Marketplace) and act as the senior analytical authority in that space. You will partner with Directors and VPs across Business, Operations, Product, and Engineering to shape long-term strategy, influence investment decisions, and ensure our roadmap is grounded in rigorous, scalable measurement frameworks.

Our Data Team is dedicated to empowering top-quality decision-making. Do you want to know how? Visit our Tech Radar to learn about the technologies we use at Preply!

What you’ll be doing:


Develop and formalize a strategic understanding of the key value drivers of your domain, including user behavior, marketplace dynamics, pricing, and long-term unit economics — and translate these into actionable decision frameworks used by leadership.
Define the long-term measurement for your domain, identifying structural growth levers and step-change opportunities beyond incremental optimization.
Lead deep analytical investigations into customer behavior and retention dynamics, reframing ambiguous business questions into structured analyses that drive step-change improvements in long-term value.
Own impact modeling and scenario analysis for major product initiatives, enabling quarterly and annual planning decisions through robust forecasting, trade-off analysis, and risk assessment.
Own the definition and governance of key metrics within your domain, ensuring clarity, consistency, and alignment with company-level objectives.
Design the analytics programme in your domain and execute, and evaluate large-scale analysis to test new ideas and measure their effectiveness in driving business outcomes.
Act as a senior thought partner to business, operations, product, engineering, and data leaders, driving alignment across teams and elevating analytical standards across the organization.
Lead cross-domain analytical initiatives (e.g., forecasting, scenario modeling, performance optimization).


What you need to succeed:


Demonstrated track record of materially influencing business, operations or product strategy in complex environments, with clear examples of company-level impact.
Expertise in strategic analytics within a business pillar, ensuring consistent KPI definitions and analytical standards across teams. Resolving ambiguity across business and technical stakeholders, balances rapid reporting with deeper strategic research, and delivers decision-ready insights that clarify trade-offs and shape high-impact initiatives, even when data is imperfect or incomplete.
Strong understanding of subscription and marketplace economics, including LTV modeling, cohort dynamics, retention mechanics, pricing trade-offs, and long-term value optimization.
Strong technical proficiency (e.g., SQL, Python) with the ability to review and elevate the analytical work of others, set coding standards, and design scalable analytical workflows.
Curiosity, problem-solving and critical-thinking skills, as well as the ability to proactively identify and address challenges.
Ability to influence Directors and VP-level stakeholders, navigate conflicting incentives, and drive clarity in high-stakes strategic discussions.
Interest in the bigger picture, feeling excited to impact the product roadmap and strategy.
Experience mentoring and technically guiding senior data analyst, contributing to the overall growth and maturity of the data organization.


Nice to have:


Background in 2-sided marketplaces or digital businesses (B2C, B2B2C).
Experience in leading the application of advanced analytical approaches (e.g., forecasting, scenario modeling, performance optimization) to drive structural improvements across a business pillar. Aligning analytical outputs with unified KPI frameworks, evaluating opportunity costs and long-term impact, and synthesizing cross-functional insights into strategic recommendations.
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI).
Master's degree or PhD in a quantitative field.
Demonstrated experience developing senior ICs and contributing to raising the analytical bar across teams.


Why you’ll love it at Preply:


An open, collaborative, dynamic and diverse culture;
A generous monthly allowance for lessons on Preply.com, Learning & Development budget and time off for your self-development;
A competitive financial package with equity, leave allowance and health insurance;
Not in Barcelona? We offer an attractive relocation package to join us in our Preply Barcelona Hub
Access to free mental health support platforms;
Access to Gympass-partnered wellness and gym centers throughout Spain to promote and support well-being and physical health;
The opportunity to unlock the potential of learners and tutors through language learning and teaching in 175 countries (and counting!).


Our Principles


Care to change the world - We are passionate about our work and care deeply about its impact to be life changing.
We do it for learners - For both Preply and tutors, learners are why we do what we do. Every day we focus on empowering tutors to deliver an exceptional learning experience.
Keep perfecting - To create an outstanding customer experience, we focus on simplicity, smoothness, and enjoyment, continually perfecting it as every detail matters.
Now is the time - In a fast-paced world, it matters how quickly we act. Now is the time to make great things happen.
Disciplined execution - What makes us disciplined is the excellence in our execution. We set clear goals, focus on what matters, and utilize our resources efficiently.
Dive deep - We leverage business acumen and curiosity to investigate disparities between numbers and stories, unlocking meaningful insights to guide our decisions.
Growth mindset - We proactively seek growth opportunities and believe today's best performance becomes tomorrow's starting point. We humbly embrace feedback and learn from setbacks.
Raise the bar - We raise our performance standards continuously, alongside each new hire and promotion. We build diverse and high-performing teams that can make a real difference.
Challenge, disagree and commit - We value open and candid communication, even when we don’t fully agree. We speak our minds, challenge when necessary, and fully commit to decisions once made.
One Preply - We prioritize collaboration, inclusion, and the success of our team over personal ambitions. Together, we support and celebrate each other's progress.


Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion

Preply.com is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people of diverse backgrounds can thrive. We believe that the presence of different opinions and viewpoints is a key ingredient for our success as a multicultural Ed-Tech company. That means that Preply will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age or veteran status.

We power people’s progress.

At Preply, we’re all about creating life-changing learning experiences. We help people discover the magic of the perfect tutor, craft a personalised learning journey, and stay motivated to keep growing. Our approach is human-led, tech-enabled - and it’s creating real impact.

We’ve just reached unicorn status with a $150M Series D, accelerating our vision to transform education through human-led, AI-enhanced learning. Today, 100,000+ tutors teach 90+ languages to learners in 180 countries - and we’re only getting started. As a category-defining company, we’re shaping what the future of learning looks like at global scale.

Every Preply lesson sparks change, fuels ambition, and drives progress that matters. Joining Preply means helping define the future of education at global scale, and building something that truly matters for millions of people, every day.

Meet the team!

At Preply, data is at the heart of every decision we make. We run hundreds of analysis to continually optimize our product, each with its own analytical and tracking challenges. The complexity of our subscription model, along with the unique dynamics of tutor-learner interactions, offers an exciting opportunity for those looking to make a real impact.

As a Staff Data Analyst , you will own the data strategy for a critical product domain (e.g., Growth, Retention, Marketplace) and act as the senior analytical authority in that space. You will partner with Directors and VPs across Business, Operations, Product, and Engineering to shape long-term strategy, influence investment decisions, and ensure our roadmap is grounded in rigorous, scalable measurement frameworks.

Our Data Team is dedicated to empowering top-quality decision-making. Do you want to know how? Visit our Tech Radar to learn about the technologies we use at Preply!

What you’ll be doing:

  • Develop and formalize a strategic understanding of the key value drivers of your domain, including user behavior, marketplace dynamics, pricing, and long-term unit economics — and translate these into actionable decision frameworks used by leadership.
  • Define the long-term measurement for your domain, identifying structural growth levers and step-change opportunities beyond incremental optimization.
  • Lead deep analytical investigations into customer behavior and retention dynamics, reframing ambiguous business questions into structured analyses that drive step-change improvements in long-term value.
  • Own impact modeling and scenario analysis for major product initiatives, enabling quarterly and annual planning decisions through robust forecasting, trade-off analysis, and risk assessment.
  • Own the definition and governance of key metrics within your domain, ensuring clarity, consistency, and alignment with company-level objectives.
  • Design the analytics programme in your domain and execute, and evaluate large-scale analysis to test new ideas and measure their effectiveness in driving business outcomes.
  • Act as a senior thought partner to business, operations, product, engineering, and data leaders, driving alignment across teams and elevating analytical standards across the organization.
  • Lead cross-domain analytical initiatives (e.g., forecasting, scenario modeling, performance optimization).

  • Demonstrated track record of materially influencing business, operations or product strategy in complex environments, with clear examples of company-level impact.
  • Expertise in strategic analytics within a business pillar, ensuring consistent KPI definitions and analytical standards across teams. Resolving ambiguity across business and technical stakeholders, balances rapid reporting with deeper strategic research, and delivers decision-ready insights that clarify trade-offs and shape high-impact initiatives, even when data is imperfect or incomplete.
  • Strong understanding of subscription and marketplace economics, including LTV modeling, cohort dynamics, retention mechanics, pricing trade-offs, and long-term value optimization.
  • Strong technical proficiency (e.g., SQL, Python) with the ability to review and elevate the analytical work of others, set coding standards, and design scalable analytical workflows.
  • Curiosity, problem-solving and critical-thinking skills, as well as the ability to proactively identify and address challenges.
  • Ability to influence Directors and VP-level stakeholders, navigate conflicting incentives, and drive clarity in high-stakes strategic discussions.
  • Interest in the bigger picture, feeling excited to impact the product roadmap and strategy.
  • Experience mentoring and technically guiding senior data analyst, contributing to the overall growth and maturity of the data organization.

Title: Senior Game Data Analyst - Paper.io 2
Description:
About Voodoo

Founded in 2013, Voodoo is a tech company that creates mobile games and apps with a mission to entertain the world. Gathering 800 employees, 7 billion downloads, and over 200 million active users, Voodoo is the #3 mobile publisher worldwide in terms of downloads after Google and Meta. Our portfolio includes chart-topping games like Mob Control, Paper.io2 and Hole.io, alongside popular apps such as BeReal and Wizz.

This position can be full remote within CET time ±3h.

Team

Our Gaming team is made up of hybrid-casual, casual, and mid-core experts. Our 'Core' games team supports internal and external studios worldwide in creating, developing, and launching new hit games, whilst our 'Live' games team focuses on delivering higher engagement on our existing and successful games.

Joining our Live games team means collaborating with gaming industry experts on globally renowned, enduring games while embracing exciting new entrepreneurial ventures. With over seven billion downloads worldwide and a portfolio of more than 10 resilient hybrid games generating more than $20m per year, we are the world's largest and most successful hybrid publisher.

You will be joining one of our Live Studios, working on Paper.io 2.

With Paper.io2, we’re building an iconic game that players will never forget!

We're a small team of excellent people who take care of everything from ideas to testing, and we intend to grow while maintaining our high standards.

Role


Autonomously take ownership of the A/B testing cycle on Paper.io 2, validating feature values, refining key parameters, and optimizing monetization to drive core KPIs.
Lead analysis of large, complex datasets to uncover player behaviors and growth opportunities.
Translate data insights into clear, actionable recommendations that shape game design and the product roadmap.
Collaborate closely with designers, product managers, and developers to embed data-driven decisions throughout the game lifecycle.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports that keep the team aligned on critical metrics.


Profile


4+ years in analytical roles within gaming, comfortable working with product teams.
Skilled in SQL and either R or Python; experience with complex, large datasets.
Strong grasp of A/B testing and statistical analysis methods.
Autonomous and pragmatic problem solver with a sharp analytical mind and attention to detail.
Thrives in fast-paced live-ops/product-driven environments with rapid iteration.
Creative thinker who challenges assumptions and sees beyond the obvious.
Player-first mindset with genuine interest in games and player behavior.
Excellent communicator who turns data into compelling, actionable stories.
Collaborative team player who adapts quickly to change and ambiguity.


Benefits


Best-in-class compensation
Other benefits based on your country of residence

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción de la empresa

MSX International es un líder global en servicios para el sector de automoción, presente en más de 80 países. Desde nuestra sede en Barcelona trabajamos con diferentes fabricantes internacionales para ofrecer soluciones innovadoras que impulsan la eficiencia, la calidad y el rendimiento de sus operaciones.

Si buscas un entorno dinámico, analítico, internacional y orientado a la mejora continua, este es tu sitio.

Descripción del empleo

Como Analista de Datos, serás responsable de gestionar, analizar y transformar datos clave relacionados con proyectos del sector automoción. Tu misión será convertir grandes volúmenes de datos en insights claros y accionables que apoyen la toma de decisiones, la optimización de procesos y el rendimiento general del negocio.

Requisitos


Realizar análisis diarios, semanales y mensuales utilizando herramientas como Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Identificar tendencias, variaciones y oportunidades de mejora
Preparar informes avanzados alineados con KPIs y requisitos del proyecto
Mantener, actualizar y asegurar la calidad de la documentación y reportes en herramientas como SharePoint y Office365
Identificar patrones en los datos y proponer acciones correctivas u oportunidades de optimización
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para convertir insights en acciones concretas
Garantizar el cumplimiento de estándares internos en informes y dashboards
Presentar análisis, conclusiones e información relevante a equipos internos y dirección
Asegurar la precisión, consistencia y calidad de los datos analizados
Proponer mejoras continuas en procesos, metodologías y flujos de análisis


Requisitos

Conocimientos y habilidades


Habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para trabajar con equipos diversos
Enfoque resolutivo para problemas complejos
Capacidad para gestionar prioridades y trabajar con plazos ajustados
Dominio de Tableau, Power BI y Excel
Conocimientos de sistemas de gestión de datos, minería de datos, procesos ETL y reporting automatizado
Competencias en análisis estadístico y modelos de rendimiento
Experiencia con herramientas de automatización (RPA) es un plus
Conocimiento de Microsoft Dynamics (deseable)


Formación


Grado en Ingeniería, Economía, Estadística, Matemáticas o formación equivalente


Experiencia


Experiencia previa demostrable como Analista de Datos


Idiomas


Español nativo
Inglés B2 (escrito y hablado)


Información adicional

Qué ofrecemos


Puesto permanente con contrato indefinido desde el primer día.
Política de teletrabajo de hasta el 50% de la jornada mensual tras el periodo de adaptación.
Formar parte de un gran equipo de profesionales en una compañía internacional consolidada.
Obtener una visión global del sector automoción, interactuando con clientes, proveedores, consultores y concesionarios.
Salario: 23.000 euros brutos anuales.
Acceso a MSX Bazaar, el portal de compras colectivas con grandes descuentos para empleados.
Posibilidad de adherirte al Plan de Retribución Flexible (seguro médico, tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, formación...).
23 días laborables de vacaciones al año según convenio y 3 días por año completo trabajado.
Incentivos adicionales por programas de recomendación interna, formadores internos, detección de nuevas oportunidades de negocio, etc.


¿Crees que eres la persona ideal para este puesto? ¡Queremos conocerte! Envíanos tu candidatura ahora.

MSX es un empleador que promueve la igualdad de oportunidades y alienta positivamente las solicitudes de candidatos debidamente cualificados y elegibles, independientemente de su sexo, raza, discapacidad, edad, orientación sexual, cambio de sexo, religión o creencias, estado civil, embarazo o maternidad. Como usuarios del programa de confianza en la discapacidad, garantizamos entrevistar a todos los candidatos discapacitados que cumplan los criterios mínimos para la vacante.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Scopely is seeking a highly motivated Analytics Engineer to join our Strategy & Business Operations team in Barcelona on a 2 day a week hybrid basis!

At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day - whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing, and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people worldwide daily.

The Strategy and Business Operations team works closely with the Senior Leadership of Scopely to answer critical business-related questions. By acting as a strategic partner, the team translates complex data into actionable insights that drive the company's decision-making process and long-term growth.

What You’ll Do


Lead and collaborate with engineers, product teams, and analysts to develop self-serve insight products that are responsive, precise, and scalable at petabyte levels
Oversee the entire data product lifecycle, including designing new tracking plans, developing data models, ELT pipelines, and self-serve insight products like Looker explores and dashboards
Act as a guardian of data quality, ensuring consistent metric reporting across a wide range of data products
Utilize cutting-edge technology stack including BigQuery, dbt and Looker
Provide technical leadership and partner with our stakeholders and their teams to enhance their daily operations and product success.
Bring expertise in a specific part of the data stack and lifecycle while contributing broadly across all aspects
Mentor team members and drive the adoption of best practices within the team


What We’re Looking For


A strong passion for solving complex analytical puzzles
Proven ability to translate business needs into advanced analytics solutions
Deep expertise with the modern data stack (BigQuery, Snowflake, dbt, Looker, Airflow, GitHub, Terraform, command line-based workflows)
Exceptional SQL skills coupled with at least 3-4 years of relevant experience in data analytics or engineering roles
Demonstrate a solid proficiency in Python, with the ability to write clean, maintainable scripts to automate data workflows, perform exploratory data analysis and integrate various data sources within our modern tech stack.
Solid understanding of data modeling principles (e.g., Kimball methodology, star schemas)
A high valuation of business context and the drive to achieve technical excellence
An inclination towards collaboration and pride in contributing to high-functioning teams
Enjoyment in working within fast-paced, growth-oriented environments
A sense of fun at work, with extracurricular interests like Ping Pong or Mario Kart seen as a bonus!


At Scopely, we create games for everyone - and want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us & embrace the adventure!

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

This role will have access to sensitive and/or confidential employee or customer personally identifiable information (PII) and/or Company financial information, and therefore will be subject to background checks upon receiving a conditional offer of employment.

About Scopely

Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.

Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.

Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.

Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/.

Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com.

Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice.

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

  • Demonstrate a solid proficiency in Python, with the ability to write clean, maintainable scripts to automate data workflows, perform exploratory data analysis and integrate various data sources within our modern tech stack.
  • Solid understanding of data modeling principles (e.g., Kimball methodology, star schemas)
  • A high valuation of business context and the drive to achieve technical excellence
  • An inclination towards collaboration and pride in contributing to high-functioning teams
  • Enjoyment in working within fast-paced, growth-oriented environments
  • A sense of fun at work, with extracurricular interests like Ping Pong or Mario Kart seen as a bonus!

At Scopely, we create games for everyone - and want to ensure that the people behind our games reflect that! We are committed to creating a diverse, supportive work environment where everyone is treated with respect. We are committed to providing equal employment opportunities and welcome individuals from all backgrounds to join us & embrace the adventure!

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

This role will have access to sensitive and/or confidential employee or customer personally identifiable information (PII) and/or Company financial information, and therefore will be subject to background checks upon receiving a conditional offer of employment.

About Scopely

Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.

Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.

Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.

Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/.

Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
As one of our Games Data Scientists you will be the business facing masterminds who help turn business questions into actionable insights. You research and analyze player behaviour, and come up with recommendations. Data Scientists do this by listening to team members, understanding context and challenging business ideas. Data Scientists use diverse techniques - frequentist and Bayesian statistics, machine learning, exploratory and explanatory data analysis, causal inference, data visualization, monte carlo modelling, econometric analysis, etc. Such broad requirements call for the ability to learn quickly, work efficiently with peers and communicate data clearly and effectively. Games Data Scientists are true visionaries who support business decisions with data and in-depth analytics.

You will have the opportunity to work with large and complex data sets, with the autonomy to make a huge impact on the success of our games. You will also be working as part of an experienced and highly skilled team of 20 with opportunities to learn and develop.

What You'll Do
Discuss with stakeholders requirements for analysis
Run exploratory data analysis and turn it into questions which can be answered with analytical techniques
Use simple analytics, statistical or causal inference, machine learning or any other techniques to answer questions and address problems
Communicate results clearly and effectively
Take care of unclear and ambiguous requirements
Communicate complex ideas and analyses in a simple way
Work independently on complex projects
Be willing to acquire new skills and learn new methodologies, whether related to stakeholder management, communication or data science
Be able to use diverse data science tools and approaches


What We're Looking For
A degree or equivalent work experience in data driven field
Ability to use visualization techniques for communicating data and analysis
Experience of using any of the following to answer business or scientific questions -statistics, mathematics, machine learning, econometrics, causal techniques, monte carlo modelling, etc.
R/Python experience
Knowledge and experience of SQL


Why Product Madness?

As part of the Aristocrat family, we share their mission of bringing joy to life through the power of play, with a world-class team who creates top-grossing, leading titles in the social casino genre, including Heart of Vegas, Lightning Link, Cashman Casino. With 800 team members across the globe, Product Madness is headquartered in London, with offices in Barcelona, Gdańsk, Lviv, Montreal and a remote team spanning the USA, making us a truly global powerhouse.

We live by our People First principle. Regardless of where, when, or how they work, our team members have opportunities to elevate their careers, and grow alongside us. We take pride in fostering an inclusive culture, where our people are encouraged to be their very best, every day. But don’t just take our word for it. In 2024, we made the Global Inspiring Workplace Awards list, and won a bronze award at the Stevies for Great Employers in the ‘Employer of the Year - Media and Entertainment’ category.

So, what’s stopping you?

Travel Expectations

None

Additional Information

At this time, we are unable to sponsor work visas for this position. Candidates must be authorized to work in the job posting location for this position on a full-time basis without the need for current or future visa sponsorship.

Title: Data Analyst (BigQuery, Looker, SQL)
Description:
En Grupo Aubay buscamos un/a Analista de Datos con buen nivel de SQL para participar en todo el ciclo analítico: obtención de datos desde SAP y otras fuentes, transformación en BigQuery, modelado para reporting y creación de dashboards en Looker.




La persona seleccionada tendrá un rol muy orientado a negocio dentro del área de Supply Chain y con exposición a tecnologías modernas como Google Cloud Platform.




¿Cuáles serán tus principales funciones?

Ingesta, análisis y comprensión de datos procedentes de SAP, sistemas legacy y GCP
Transformación de datos bajo arquitectura Medallion (Bronze, Silver, Gold)
Diseño y mantenimiento de modelos de datos dimensionales (Kimball)
Desarrollo de pipelines en Dataform
Creación de dashboards reutilizables en Looker
Control y validación de calidad de datos
Traducción de necesidades de negocio a soluciones analíticas
Documentación técnica y funcional de modelos y datasets




Requisitos

1–2 años de experiencia como Data Analyst o perfil similar
SQL avanzado
Experiencia en modelado dimensional (Kimball)
Conocimientos de Python
Experiencia o conocimiento en GCP (especialmente BigQuery)
Nivel alto de inglés (imprescindible)
Tecnologías del entorno: GCP (BigQuery), Dataform, Looker, GitHub, Modelado Kimball




Se ofrece

Participación en proyectos de analítica avanzada en Supply Chain
Horario de 9h a 18h
Modelo híbrido de trabajo
Trabajo con equipos multidisciplinares internacionales
Retribución flexible

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Descripción oferta

Desde la Dirección de NEGOCIO, precisamos incorporar un/a DATA ANALYST en ANÁLISIS DE NEGOCIO, ubicado en BARCELONA.

Su misión será facilitar a los equipos de Negocio información de valor sobre la evolución del negocio y los clientes de VidaCaixa, facilitando la obtención de insights y conclusiones a partir de la explotación analítica de datos. La persona que se incorpore combinará el conocimiento de técnicas y herramientas de explotación de datos con fuertes capacidades analíticas y conocimiento de negocio.

¿Cuál será tu propósito dentro de VidaCaixa?


Desarrollo y divulgación de análisis e informes que permitan comprender la evolución del negocio y faciliten la obtención de insights.
Análisis en profundidad que faciliten la toma de decisiones y acompañen la estrategia de negocio: evolución de las ventas y las cancelaciones por producto, impacto de campañas, lanzamiento de nuevos productos, simuladores y creación de escenarios, valoración de oportunidades y potencial de negocio, …
Incorporación de la visión cliente que permita entender las necesidades y comportamientos de los clientes a partir la analítica avanzada de datos.
Desarrollo de cuadros de mando (QlikSense) y procesos automatizados que permitan innovar y optimizar la explotación y acceso transversal a los datos.
Innovación y mejora continua en las formas de explotar y visualizar la información, explorando el potencial de nuevas herramientas y la IA.
Trabajar juntamente con los equipos de negocio para la identificación de nuevas oportunidades a partir de los datos.
Conocimiento de las bases de datos y el sistema informacional a partir del que se explotan los datos de clientes.



Requisitos:

Eres el talento que estamos buscando si cuentas con…


Formación universitaria superior (Matemáticas, Estadística, Ingeniería, Informática, Física…) o cualquier formación con foco en especialidad analítica.
Nivel avanzado en programación SQL para el análisis y transformación de bases de datos.
Experiencia previa como Data Analyst o analista de Business Intelligence: análisis de perfil de clientes, seguimientos de negocio, definición de indicadores, etc.
Eres una persona con iniciativa, organizada y orientada a resultados.
Cuentas con capacidad analítica para la extracción, comunicación y presentación de conclusiones a los responsables de negocio a partir de los datos.
Facilidad para trabajar en equipo y en proyectos con equipos transversales.
Curiosidad, predisposición a la innovación, aprendizaje y mejora continua.
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia en herramientas de diseño de cuadros de mando y visualización de datos (QlikSense, PowerBI, Tableau).
Se valorará de forma positiva experiencia previa en el sector bancario o asegurador.



Beneficios

Sabemos que harás un gran trabajo, por eso hemos pensado en estos beneficios para ti…


Contrato indefinido
Flexibilidad horaria de entrada (7:30 a 9:30) y en la comida (puedes emplear entre un mínimo de 45 minutos y un máximo de 2 horas) para que puedas autogestionar tu tiempo
Plan de acogida y seguimiento de tu desarrollo profesional, con evaluaciones 360 y planes de desarrollo individuales.
Acceso a una plataforma online de formación, con un amplio catálogo autoformativo, para que tu aprendizaje sea continuo.
Todos los viernes con jornada intensiva para que puedas disfrutar más del fin de semana.
Del 15 de junio al 15 de septiembre jornada intensiva TODOS los días, porque sabemos que es verano y quieres disfrutar más tiempo de las horas de sol
Modelo de trabajo híbrido. Porque creemos que pasar tiempo en la oficina es un factor clave para desarrollar la creatividad, y disfrutar en equipo.
Fruta, porque es importante cuidarse. ¡Mens sana, in corpore sano!
29 días hábiles de vacaciones. ¡Desconectar, para volver a conectar!
Plan de Beneficios: plan de pensiones, seguro de salud, seguro de vida, ticket restaurante
Retribución flexible aplicado a guardería y/o transporte.



Proceso

¿Cómo será el proceso de selección?


Recibimos y validamos tu solicitud
Evaluación telefónica con especialista de selección
Realización de informe
Entrevista con el/la responsable de departamento
Entrevista personal con HRBP
¡Y no lo olvides, te daremos feedback!

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

You will be at the heart of Glovo's Global Logistics and Operations team, owning the data work behind how we design, test, and optimize the order compensation structures that reward our riders across 23 countries. This is one of the highest impact areas in the company, rider payments are our largest operational cost, and every model you build or experiment you run translates directly into business outcomes at scale.

The environment is fast moving. Priorities shift and ad hoc requests come in regularly. This internship is built for someone curious, hands-on, and eager to learn how real operational decisions get made with data. You will be paired with experienced analysts who will mentor you along the way.

THE JOURNEY


Support the team in modelling and reviewing order payment structures that improve engagement and operational efficiency.
You will be using SQL to query large datasets to surface trends, answer business questions quickly, and build the analytical foundation for strategic decisions.
Translate findings into action. You will help present insights to non-technical stakeholders in a way that is concise and convincing.
Support the design and running of experiments that validate setting changes before global rollout. You will be exposed to the full cycle: design, execution, and analysis.
Leverage AI in your workflow. You will actively incorporate AI tools (agents, copilots, and LLM-assisted analysis) into your day to day work to move faster and work smarter.
Monitor and iterate. Track KPIs, spot anomalies, and make data driven adjustments on an ongoing basis.
Understand the rider perspective to ensure models are fair, locally relevant, and operationally sound.


Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE


Recently completed a degree or master's in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or another quantitative field.
SQL is essential. You can write queries to pull and filter data, join tables, and aggregate results on your own.
Python familiarity. You can use pandas (or similar) to manipulate data and automate simple tasks.
Excel / spreadsheet familiarity. You know your way around formulas, pivot tables, and structuring data cleanly.
Strong problem-solving instincts. You can take an ambiguous question, break it down, and work toward a clear answer without waiting to be told exactly how.
Fluent English is a must. You communicate clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Nice-to-haves


Hands-on experience leveraging AI in your workflow: copilots, agents, or LLM-assisted analysis used to move faster and work smarter.
Exposure to data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
Any exposure to experimentation (A/B testing, switchback tests, or similar).


We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Additional Information

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

YOUR MISSION

You will be at the heart of Glovo's Global Logistics and Operations team, owning the data work behind how we design, test, and optimize the order compensation structures that reward our riders across 23 countries. This is one of the highest impact areas in the company, rider payments are our largest operational cost, and every model you build or experiment you run translates directly into business outcomes at scale.

The environment is fast moving. Priorities shift and ad hoc requests come in regularly. This internship is built for someone curious, hands-on, and eager to learn how real operational decisions get made with data. You will be paired with experienced analysts who will mentor you along the way.

THE JOURNEY

  • Support the team in modelling and reviewing order payment structures that improve engagement and operational efficiency.
  • You will be using SQL to query large datasets to surface trends, answer business questions quickly, and build the analytical foundation for strategic decisions.
  • Translate findings into action. You will help present insights to non-technical stakeholders in a way that is concise and convincing.
  • Support the design and running of experiments that validate setting changes before global rollout. You will be exposed to the full cycle: design, execution, and analysis.
  • Leverage AI in your workflow. You will actively incorporate AI tools (agents, copilots, and LLM-assisted analysis) into your day to day work to move faster and work smarter.
  • Monitor and iterate. Track KPIs, spot anomalies, and make data driven adjustments on an ongoing basis.
  • Understand the rider perspective to ensure models are fair, locally relevant, and operationally sound.

Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE

  • Recently completed a degree or master's in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or another quantitative field.
  • SQL is essential. You can write queries to pull and filter data, join tables, and aggregate results on your own.
  • Python familiarity. You can use pandas (or similar) to manipulate data and automate simple tasks.
  • Excel / spreadsheet familiarity. You know your way around formulas, pivot tables, and structuring data cleanly.
  • Strong problem-solving instincts. You can take an ambiguous question, break it down, and work toward a clear answer without waiting to be told exactly how.
  • Fluent English is a must. You communicate clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Nice-to-haves

  • Hands-on experience leveraging AI in your workflow: copilots, agents, or LLM-assisted analysis used to move faster and work smarter.
  • Exposure to data visualization tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
  • Any exposure to experimentation (A/B testing, switchback tests, or similar).

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves

  • 🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
  • 💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
  • 🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
  • 🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
  • 👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
  • 🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.

Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Additional Information

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
VINCLE, consultora tecnológica en expansión, busca incorporar para un importante proyecto con gran multinacional de gran consumo, un Data Analyst Junior.




Te incorporarás a uno de los proyectos más pioneros del mercado, basado en la automatización de la demanda y de los suministros. Centrado en elaborar la previsión de la demanda y asegurar que el nivel de stock es el que se establece desde la dirección del departamento. Todo centralizado en departamentos de Supply y Demand.




🚀 FUNCIONES PRINCIPALES:

- Responsable del seguimiento y análisis de los KPI's clave del proyecto: Forecast Accuracy, Cambios, OSA, Freshness, Ventas.

- Interlocución con los departamentos de sistemas y gestión de incidencias relacionadas con el Forecast como principal input de la generación de los pedidos.

- Mantenimiento de los parámetros necesarios para el correcto dimensionamiento de los pedidos.

- Animación y seguimiento del forecast así como tareas que garantizan el nivel de servicio.




🔎 ¿Qué buscamos?:

Formación Superior en ADE, Ingenieria Industrial, Ingenieria Quimica, Grado en Matemáticas o similar.
Deseable haber realizado Erasmus o estancias en el extranjero por estudios o trabajo.
Experiencia de 1 año en puesto similar sobre todo en empresa de gran consumo y en análisis de datos.
Se valorará experiencia previa en departamentos de logística, demand.
Dominio avanzado de excel (tablas dinámicas, macros, formulación, ...)
Muy valorable conocimientos de herramientas de análisis estadístico: Matlab, SAS, Revolution Analytics, BI, ...
Capacidad de análisis, gestión y resolución.
Actitud positiva ante situaciones complejas.
Alto nivel de aprendizaje y compromiso con el trabajo.
Persona con entusiasmo, ganas de trabajar y actitud positiva.




✨ ¿Qué ofrecemos?

Horario: 40 horas semanale de Lunes a Viernes: L – J 9:00h a 18:30h y V 8:30h a 15:00h
Contrato Indefinido.
Ubicación del puesto: centro Barcelona.
Incorporación inmediata.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Guesty is the all-in-one platform helping hospitality businesses around the world automate, optimize, and scale their operations. We’re a fast-growing global startup transforming the industry — from enhancing guest experiences to driving business growth.

With a powerful suite of features and integrations with 150+ industry partners, including Airbnb, Vrbo, Booking.com, Expedia, and Google Travel, Guesty empowers property managers to deliver exceptional guest experiences while running efficient, data-driven operations.

Today, we’re proud to have 800+ team members across 16 countries, all working together to shape the future of hospitality technology.

We are looking for a skilled Data Analyst to join our Analytics team. In this role, you will take ownership of marketing analytics, partnering closely with the marketing leadership team to monitor campaign performance, track KPIs, and provide insights that shape marketing strategy. You will own the Marketing department’s dashboards and data pipelines, analyze key lifecycle and campaign data, and surface insights that directly inform decision-making.

You will work primarily with SQL and Looker to query, structure, and present data and will help refine the analytics framework for our marketing efforts across acquisition and engagement.

Responsibilities:


Serve as the go-to person for all analytical aspects related to marketing performance.
Develop, refine, and maintain metrics to monitor marketing success across channels such as paid search, paid social, email, and organic.
Maintain and improve existing dashboards and create new ones.
Define, build, and track KPIs (e.g., CAC, LTV, ROAS), presenting results and trends to senior stakeholders.


Requirements:


2+ years of experience as a Data Analyst or Business Analyst, preferably in a SaaS company.
Background in Marketing analytics.
BSc/BA in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, or Industrial Engineering.
Strong analytical skills and advanced proficiency in SQL (essential).
Solid knowledge of reporting and data visualization techniques (essential); experience with Looker is a plus.
Team player with excellent communication and presentation skills; fluent in English.
Ability to communicate complex data insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Highly detail-oriented, with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.


Preferred By Not Required


Experience with HubSpot, Google Analytics, Google Ads, and Facebook Ads Manager.
Experience as a business analyst in a SaaS B2B company.
Background in statistical research.


Guesty is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, disability, veteran status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Would you like to join an international atmosphere and be involved in projects across Europe and with impact worldwide having the opportunity to consolidate your career? This is your opportunity!




🚀 We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team!




The Data Analyst will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data models and Power BI dashboards based on Apptio Cost & Financial Data Lake (CFDL) to support Aftersales performance, cost transparency, and financial steering. The role acts as a bridge between Aftersales business stakeholders, Finance, and IT/Data teams, ensuring high‑quality, actionable insights.




🔑 Key Responsibilities




- Develop and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards using data sourced from Apptio CFDL.

- Build and optimize data models (facts, dimensions, measures) aligned with Aftersales KPIs.

- Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reconciliation with financial and operational sources.

- Perform ad‑hoc analyses to support decision‑making in Aftersales and Finance. Aftersales & Financial Steering

- Translate Aftersales business needs into analytical requirements and KPIs.

- Support cost transparency, profitability, and performance tracking across Aftersales activities.

- Collaborate with Finance to align reporting with financial controlling and cost allocation logic.

- Identify trends, risks, and improvement opportunities through data insights. Apptio / CFDL Ownership

- Act as subject matter expert for Apptio CFDL data structures and logic.

- Coordinate with IT / Data Platform teams on data ingestion, transformations, and enhancements. - Ensure correct usage of Apptio data for reporting and analysis purposes. Stakeholder Management

- Work closely with Aftersales stakeholders to understand reporting needs and priorities.

- Present insights in a clear, business‑oriented way to non‑technical audiences.

- Support users with Power BI adoption, interpretation of reports, and best practices.




🛠 Technical Skills




- Strong experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, performance optimization).

- Solid understanding of Apptio / TBM concepts, ideally with exposure to CFDL.

- Experience working with financial and cost data.

- Good knowledge of SQL and data analysis concepts.

- Understanding of data quality, controls, and reconciliation processes.




🌍 Business & Soft Skills




- Strong analytical and problem‑solving mindset.

- Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.

- Experience working with cross‑functional teams (Aftersales, Finance, IT).

- Structured, detail‑oriented, and proactive way of working.

- Strong communication skills in English (Spanish/German is a plus). Nice to Have

- Experience in Aftersales, Automotive, or Shared Services environments.

- Knowledge of SAP (FI/CO, Aftersales‑related data).

- Experience with cost allocation models and profitability analysis.

- Previous work in international or multi‑country setups. Key Interfaces

- Aftersales Business Teams - Finance / Controlling - IT / Data Platform / Apptio Teams

- Power BI & Reporting Communities Success Indicators

- High adoption and satisfaction of Aftersales Power BI reports.

- Reliable, reconciled, and trusted data from Apptio CFDL.

- Clear contribution to Aftersales cost transparency and performance steering.

- Reduced manual reporting and ad‑hoc data requests.




⭐ Within our benefits as employee, we provide:




60% Mobile work
30 working days of holidays
Annual Training Plan

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
En Volkswagen Group Services Barcelona estamos buscando un/ nuevo/a Compañero/a que dará soporte directo en análisis de datos de negocio al responsable de área. Proporcionará información estructurada, informes periódicos e insights relevantes sobre el rendimiento de los servicios. Su labor se centrará en el análisis de indicadores clave como enrollment, automatic service scheduling y la evolución de los servicios por mercado, país y modelo, así como en el mantenimiento de dashboards y herramientas de reporting en Power BI, asegurando la calidad, fiabilidad y disponibilidad de la información necesaria para la toma de decisiones del área.




Entre las funciones del puesto:

1. Soporte en el análisis de datos del servicio: Dar apoyo al responsable interno en el análisis de datos de servicios conectados (enrollment, automatic service scheduling, métricas de negocio adicionales, uso por mercado/modelo), facilitando la identificación de patrones y desviaciones relevantes.

2.Apoyo en la generación de insights accionables: Asistir al responsable interno en la extracción de conclusiones relevantes a partir de los datos, enfocadas en mejorar el negocio y la experiencia del cliente.

3. Apoyo en la definición y seguimiento de KPIs: Colaborar en la definición, mantenimiento y actualización de KPIs de segundo nivel orientados a negocio, asegurando consistencia de métricas.

4. Preparación de información para la toma de decisiones: Preparar y estructurar datos, informes y resúmenes ejecutivos como base para las decisiones del responsable del área.

5. Elaboración de informes periódicos y análisis puntuales: Ejecutar reportes recurrentes y análisis ad-hoc bajo demanda del responsable interno o de otros stakeholders del área.

6. Soporte en la gestión de Power BI y reporting: Mantener dashboards, modelos de datos y visualizaciones en Power BI bajo la dirección del responsable del área.

7. Apoyo en el control de calidad del dato: Colaborar en la revisión de fuentes de datos, identificación de inconsistencias y validación de resultados de reporting.

8. Coordinación con equipos técnicos y de datos: Alinear fuentes, definiciones de KPI y metodología con IT y equipos analíticos.

9. Preparación de presentaciones y documentación: Elaborar materiales de soporte para comités, reuniones y comunicaciones internas.




Qué esperamos de ti:

Grado universitario en Ingeniería Economía, Analítica de Datos o formación equivalente.
Valorable formación específica en gestión de proyectos data analytics o negocios digitales.
Valorable formación en Power BI y Business Intelligence (modelado de datos, DAX, visualización y gobierno del dato).
Análisis de datos, reporting avanzado o herramientas BI complementarias.
Mínimo 2 años en funciones de soporte analítico, reporting, gestión de datos o soporte operativo a áreas de negocio, preferiblemente en entornos digitales, tecnológicos o de automoción.
Inglés c1
Análisis e interpretación de datos orientados a negocio.
Definición y seguimiento de KPIs y métricas de rendimiento.
Gestión administrativa y presupuestaria básica (volúmenes, facturación, licencias).
Capacidad para estructurar información compleja y convertirla en reportes ejecutivos.
Coordinación en entornos transversales y trabajo colaborativo con múltiples áreas.
Ofimática avanzada: PowerPoint, Excel.
Business Intelligence y reporting: Power BI (nivel medio).
Gestión de trabajo y documentación: Confluence, Jira (valorable)
Conocimiento de metodologías de trabajo Agile (SAFe, Scrum)




Qué podemos ofrecerte:

Subvención diaria para la cantina.
Acceso gratuito a transporte de empresa.
Opción para participar en el Plan de Retribución Flexible, según la normativa interna de la empresa.
Acceso subvencionado a formación en idiomas.
Acceso a Plataforma de Formación E-learning.
Beneficios corporativos.
Descuentos en automóviles del grupo.
Hasta el 60% de teletrabajo

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
En Carrefour estamos transformando nuestros sistemas centrales para modernizar nuestra actividad principal, el comercio al por menor.




Si quieres ser parte de una empresa dinámica, dentro de un sector con gran potencial de desarrollo que te ofrecerá oportunidades de crecimiento profesional y personal, nos encantará tener la oportunidad de conocerte.




Buscamos un/una Data Analyst para nuestra dirección de seguros con el objetivo de análizar los datos de la compañía con eje cliente através de la minería de los datos a partir de distintas fuentes y sobre un entorno Big Data y con el objetivo de transformarlos en información accionable para la compañía y proveer a la Compañia de informes y visualizaciones de datos útiles para la toma de decisiones. Discriminar y separar los datos para ofrecer información relevante a sus clientes. La posición debe potenciar e impulsar nuevos planteamientos de análisis orientados al cliente con el objetivo de realizar aportaciones de valor a las Unidades de Negocio.




Entre sus funciones principales se encuentran:




-Tener una visión clara de negocio con el fin de ayudar a las unidades de negocio a mejorar su toma de decisiones con respecto al cliente.

- Ofrecer una visualización de los datos que permita su mejor entendimiento por sus clientes.

- Aportar análisis cuantitativos basados en el histórico de datos internos de los clientes para ofrecer previsiones de comportamiento del mismo a Negocio, con el objetivo de ajustar los esfuerzos comerciales a la tendencia del cliente Realizar aportes de valor a las unidades de negocio orientado a la mejora de las decisiones que se adopten.

- Análisis e interpretación de los resultados a partir de distintas técnicas avanzadas (regresiones, algoritmos matemáticos avanzados…) y de herramientas analíticas para detectar las causas raíz de problemas de áreas de negocio .







Nos encantaría conocerte si cuentas con:




-Titulación de Ingeniero Superior, Matemático o Estadístico.

-Experiencia de 2 años en puestos similares.

-Ingles nivel medio-alto.

-Bases Datos SQL y Data Mining.







¿Qué te ofrecemos?

Contrato indefinido

Salario competitivo (fijo + variable)

Descuento del 8% para ti y para 6 personas más en nuestras tiendas

Programas que se preocupan por tu bienestar:

Carrefour Life (actividades y eventos deportivos, descuentos y ventajas exclusivas)

Formación continua

Desarrollo profesional




Valoraremos todas las candidaturas interesadas y si eres la persona seleccionada para avanzar en el proceso, nos pondremos en contacto contigo para darte más detalles del puesto y conocer tu perfil.




En Carrefour apostamos por un espacio de trabajo idóneo para que cualquier persona con independencia de su género, orientación sexual, identidad y/o expresión de género, creencias, ideologías, origen, capacidades, entre otras, pueda desplegar todo su potencial.

Title: Data Analyst - Quality & Fraud (They/She/He)
Description:
Glovo is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 70 countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.

Job Description

If you’re here, it’s because you’re looking for an exciting ride.

A ride that will fuel up your ambitions to take on a new challenge and stretch yourself beyond your comfort zone.

We’ll deliver a non-vanilla culture built on talent, where we work to amplify the impact on millions of people, paving the way forward together.

Not your usual app. We are the fastest-growing multi-category app connecting millions of users with businesses, and couriers, offering on-demand services from more than 170,000 local restaurants, grocers and supermarkets, and high street retail stores. We operate in more than 1500 cities across 23 countries.

Together we revolutionise the way people connect with their everyday needs, from delivering essentials to connecting our ecosystem of users through innovative solutions powered by technology. For us, every day is filled with purpose.

What makes our ride unique?

🤝 Our culture and strong values.

💪 Our career development philosophy.

🤝 Our commitment to being a force for good.

We have a vision: Building the largest marketplace in your city, to give access to anything in minutes. And this is where your ride starts.

YOUR MISSION

Join Glovo's Global Operations team and become an integral part of our dynamic and challenging environment, offering numerous opportunities for growth and development.

We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join the Courier Fraud team, driving the design, monitoring, and implementation of our global anti-fraud and risk mitigation strategy.

In this role, you will be responsible for delivering data-driven insights, building detection frameworks and dashboards, and ensuring that our global integrity standards are consistently met and enforced. Your work will be central to reducing malicious activity (such as abuse of cancellations, account sharing, GPS spoofing, and payment abuse) and to guaranteeing that Glovo’s fleet remains fair and secure.

THE JOURNEY


Own and define the global courier fraud strategy across all Glovo markets, ensuring consistent implementation and continuous improvement.
Proactively address key courier-related challenges at local, regional, and global levels.
Develop scalable self-service dashboards and monitoring tools that empower local teams to act on quality-related insights.
Perform deep-dive analyses to identify the drivers of quality gaps and propose clear, actionable solutions.
Provide regular updates and reports to leadership on courier quality performance, highlighting risks and opportunities.
Develop analytical models and frameworks to monitor fraud risks, predict potential no-shows, and optimize fleet distribution.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Operations, Fraud Prevention, Product, and Data teams to align strategies and ensure impact.
Step into the shoes of the couriers—understanding their pain points and motivations—to design data-informed solutions that drive both courier satisfaction and operational excellence.


Qualifications

WHAT YOU WILL BRING TO THE RIDE


Degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Project Management, Business Analytics or any data driven domain.
+3 years of work experience providing internal teams with the necessary data to make the right decisions and proven experience on actionate those insights (this is a must).
Strong analytical and coding skills: Good in SQL and Excel proficiency (or extensive coding experience), and BI platforms experience (Looker, Tableau, PowerBI,…).
Strong strategic vision and business mindset.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. Comfortable to break down complex problems into solutions.
Comfortable working in a high-growth and high-performance environment, dealing with ambiguity while maintaining a positive mindset.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills (English is a must)
Proactive, organized, and obsessed with detail.


Individuals representing diverse profiles, and abilities, encompassing various genders, ethnicities, and backgrounds, are less likely to apply for this role if they do not possess solid experience in 100% of these areas. Even if it seems you don't meet our musts don't let it stop you, we are all about finding the best talent out there! Skills can be learned, and embracing diversity is invaluable.

We Believe Driven Talent Deserves


🌟 An enticing equity plan that lets you own a piece of the action.
💪 Top-notch private health insurance to keep you at your peak.
🍔 Monthly Glovo credit to satisfy your cravings!
💳 Discounts on transportation, food, and even kindergarten expenses.
🏊 Discounted gym memberships to keep you energized.
🏖️ Extra time off, the freedom to work from home two days a week, and the opportunity to work from anywhere for up to three weeks a year!
👪 Enhanced parental leave, and office-based nursery.
🧠 Online therapy and wellbeing benefits to ensure your mental well-being.


Here at Glovo, we thrive on diversity, we believe it enhances our teams, products, and culture. We know that the best ideas come from a mashup of brilliant diverse minds. This is why we are committed to providing equal opportunities to talent from all backgrounds - all genders, racial/diverse backgrounds, abilities, ages, sexual orientations and all other unique characteristics that make you YOU. We will encourage you to bring your authentic self to work, fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard.

Feel free to note your pronouns in your application (e.g., she/her/hers, he/him/his, they/them/theirs, etc).

So, ready to take the wheel and make this the ride of your life?

Delve into our culture by taking a peek at our Instagram and check out our Linkedin and website!

Additional Information

At Glovo, your success is defined by both results and behaviors. We hire for excellence in craft and for the Leadership Principles that shape how we think, decide, and collaborate. What you achieve matters and how you achieve it defines us. Together, they move the business forward and deliver great experiences. Learn more about our Leadership Principles here.

Title: Data Analyst - Oportunidades de futuro
Description:
¿POR QUÉ ELEGIR SNGULAR?

La gente viene a SNGULAR atraída por proyectos que usan tecnologías punteras, y se quedan por los grandes profesionales con los que trabajan. Y además porque ofrecemos esto:







🙋Personas primero: somos una empresa colaborativa orientada a las personas, donde TÚ y tu perspectiva siempre serán valoradas. Nuestro valor número uno es “Personas antes que resultados”;. ¿Suena bonito? Pues en SNGULAR, además, lo llevamos a la práctica.




📈 Crecimiento y formación continua: Tenemos un espíritu de Start-up con la infraestructura de una gran organización (ya somos 1500). Se te ofrecerán oportunidades de crecimiento profesional: aprender nuevas habilidades, obtener nuevas certificaciones, o convertirte en un/a gran Líder... ¡deja tu zona de confort de la manera que TÚ quieras!

🏋️ Beneficios increíbles (los tienes detallados al final), tenemos incluso un Wellbeing Pack para cuidar nuestro bienestar físico y mental.




Hemos obtenido el reconocimiento de ser una de las 10 mejores empresas para desarrollar una carrera profesional en España, según la prestigiosa lista LinkedIn Top Companies 2026.




Conoce todo lo que hace de Sngular un #BestPlaceToGrow




LA OPORTUNIDAD

Buscamos un/a Data Analyst para incorporarse a la comunidad de Data de Sngular y participar en proyectos de analítica avanzada, Business Intelligence y explotación del dato para clientes de diferentes sectores.




¿QUÉ HARÁS EN TU DÍA A DÍA?

Serás responsable de transformar datos complejos en conocimiento útil para el negocio, diseñando modelos analíticos, dashboards y métricas que permitan comprender mejor el rendimiento de productos, procesos y operaciones.




Trabajarás estrechamente con equipos de negocio, Data Engineering y Data Science para garantizar la calidad de la información y facilitar la toma de decisiones basada en datos.




Analizar grandes volúmenes de datos para identificar tendencias, patrones y oportunidades de mejora.
Diseñar y desarrollar dashboards, informes y visualizaciones orientadas a negocio.
Definir y construir KPIs, métricas y modelos semánticos para diferentes áreas de la organización.
Trabajar con equipos de negocio para comprender necesidades analíticas y traducirlas en soluciones de datos.
Realizar análisis exploratorios y estudios ad hoc para apoyar la toma de decisiones.
Diseñar consultas optimizadas sobre entornos de datos de gran volumen.
Garantizar la calidad, consistencia y trazabilidad de la información utilizada en reporting y analítica.
Colaborar con equipos de Data Engineering en la definición y validación de modelos de datos.
Automatizar procesos de análisis y generación de información mediante herramientas y scripts.
Participar en iniciativas de gobierno del dato y mejora continua de la calidad de la información.




¿QUÉ ES NECESARIO PARA EL PUESTO?

Experiencia de al menos 4 años como Data Analyst.
Dominio avanzado de SQL para análisis y explotación de datos.
Experiencia en Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
Experiencia trabajando con BigQuery u otras plataformas analíticas cloud.
Conocimientos de Python aplicado a análisis, automatización y tratamiento de datos.
Experiencia desarrollando dashboards e informes con Looker, Power BI o herramientas equivalentes.
Experiencia diseñando modelos analíticos y definiendo KPIs de negocio.
Capacidad para traducir necesidades de negocio en soluciones analíticas.
Conocimiento de modelado de datos y estructuras analíticas.
Experiencia trabajando con grandes volúmenes de información.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación y capacidad para interactuar con perfiles técnicos y de negocio.
Mentalidad analítica y orientación a la toma de decisiones basada en datos.




Es valorable:

Conocimiento de LookML y modelado semántico en Looker.
Experiencia en procesos ETL/ELT y herramientas de transformación de datos.
Experiencia en sectores como Retail, eCommerce, Banca, Telecomunicaciones o Salud.
Inglés.







BENEFICIOS

🎓 Formación continua: presupuesto individual, certificaciones, acceso a Udemy, clases de idiomas y programas internos.

🏠 Flexibilidad total: horario flexible y posibilidad de trabajar desde nuestros hubs o desde las instalaciones del cliente, depende del proyecto

📅 Tiempo libre adicional: 22 días de vacaciones + tu cumpleaños + 2 días para eventos técnicos + 24 y 31 de diciembre no laborables.

🌞 Wellbeing Pack: apoyo para tu bienestar físico y mental.

💵 Retribución flexible y programas de incentivos.

🎉 Eventos y dinámicas de teambuilding.

🎁 Pack de bienvenida y elección de equipo (Windows / Mac).

🚀 Bonus adicionales por recomendación, charlas o artículos técnicos.




And last, but not least: ¡porque somos muy Sngulares!💙 Tenemos Encuentro Virtual todos los viernes, fiestas, espacios donde podrás expresarte, proponer cambios y ser partícipe de ellos.







🌱 Nuestro compromiso Sngular: Como equipo comprometido con la equidad de nuestro Plan de Igualdad, en SNGULAR impulsamos una cultura plural donde el talento no tiene etiquetas y cada persona cuenta con las mismas oportunidades. Trabajamos desde el respeto, la humildad y el compañerismo, convencidos/as de que la diversidad nos fortalece.
