🌞 Wellbeing Pack: apoyo para tu bienestar físico y mental.

💵 Retribución flexible y programas de incentivos.

🎉 Eventos y dinámicas de teambuilding.

🎁 Pack de bienvenida y elección de equipo (Windows / Mac).

🚀 Bonus adicionales por recomendación, charlas o artículos técnicos.


And last, but not least: ¡porque somos muy Sngulares!💙 Tenemos Encuentro Virtual todos los viernes, fiestas, espacios donde podrás expresarte, proponer cambios y ser partícipe de ellos.



🌱 Nuestro compromiso Sngular: Como equipo comprometido con la equidad de nuestro Plan de Igualdad, en SNGULAR impulsamos una cultura plural donde el talento no tiene etiquetas y cada persona cuenta con las mismas oportunidades. Trabajamos desde el respeto, la humildad y el compañerismo, convencidos/as de que la diversidad nos fortalece.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About BizAway

Here at BizAway, we Deliver the Future of Travel. 😎

We are a solid international company with strong ambitions and great expertise. With a focus on sustainability 🌱, on a daily basis we support companies enabling them to improve their travel management through our constantly evolving services and solutions, always characterized by our tech attitude and smart and innovative processes. 🚀

We know that success comes from People and deserves to be recognized.👫Proactivity and

Reliability, Kind collaboration, and communication are the core values of our Solution Culture.

If you like challenges and would love to be part of one of the fastest-growing B2B scale-ups, then BizAway is the company you have been looking for. 🤩

We are looking for

We are looking for a driven and enthusiastic Data Analyst (m/w/d) , who desires to contribute to the growth and success of our Data & Analytics Team.

Your role in BizAway


You manage and improve our current reporting system, supporting all internal needs for Data visualization of data findings through charts, graphs, and dashboards to make our data understandable.
You learn all aspects of the travel industry from Finance, Products, Sales and Service/Operations perspective, translating data into actionable insights resulting in verifiable reporting.
By having domain expertise in our industry, you engage with Business and Strategy stakeholders to help define KPIs, including how data will be captured, shared, and leveraged across our departments.
You contribute to creating the base foundations of a comprehensive source of truth through data modeling actions


Your Basic Qualifications


Previously worked in a Tech Company
Demonstrated experience with Product Analytics tools (eg: Amplitude, Mixpanel, Pendo, Google Analytics)
Demonstrated experience with BI Data Visualization tools (Qlik preferred)
Data analysis, presentation, and visualization experience
Strong communication skills required
Ability to work alone or in a team as required
Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Engineering, Economics or a related field is preferred
English proficiency (written and spoken level B2)


We are also interested in


Knowledge of programming languages such as Python
Experience with ETL tools
Understanding of AI/ML concepts


Our offer

📄 Contract type: Permanent

🕓 Working hours: Full-time – 40h/week, from Monday to Friday

🏢 Hybrid setting, flexible working policy, and great offices in Europe

💰 Salary: Attractive compensation, including equity in the company

💼 Equity in the company (stock options plan)

✈️ A seat on a scale-up with skyrocketing growth

📈 Development of your entrepreneurial spirit, having the chance to implement real-impact business decision

🌍Multicultural and international team

🙌Collaborative and smart environment to work and learn

🍻Free coffee to kick-start your day and free beers to celebrate together

BizAway avoids any discrimination based on age, gender, sexual orientation, health status, nationality, political opinions, and religious beliefs in all decisions affecting personnel selection.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About MIGx

MIGx is a global consulting company with an exclusive focus on the healthcare and life science industries, with their particularly demanding requirements on quality and regulatory aspects. We have been managing challenges and solving problems for our clients in the areas of compliance, business processes and many others.




MIGx interdisciplinary teams from Switzerland, Spain and Georgia have been taking care of projects in the fields of M&A, Integration, Application, Data Platforms, Processes, IT management, Digital transformation, Managed services and compliance.




About the profile

We’re looking for a data-driven professional with a passion for turning both structured and unstructured data into meaningful insights. You enjoy exploring data, organizing it into clear models, and bringing it to life through compelling dashboards that deliver real business value.




Project Description

As part of this project, you will develop interactive and insightful dashboards using Tableau and/or Power BI, drawing from multiple, integrated data sources. You will join a cross-functional team at MIGx focused on delivering high-impact Data Services and scalable Data Products for our clients—primarily medium to large enterprises.

This role offers an excellent opportunity to expand your capabilities in the data domain. In addition to working on a key data visualization initiative, you will contribute to implementing modern data architecture principles—guided by best practices such as Data Mesh, Microsoft Fabric, and the Medallion Architecture—to ensure scalability, modularity, and alignment with business goals.




Responsibilities

Build semantic data models to enable efficient reporting and analytics.
Integrate and configure multiple data sources, with a focus on SQL-based systems.
Generate and maintain analytical reports tailored to business requirements.
Perform ad-hoc data analysis to support various business stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with data engineers and analysts to ensure data accuracy, performance, and usability.
Design and develop dashboards using Tableau, Power BI, or similar tools to support data-driven decision-making.




Requirements - Must have

Advanced SQL expertise and solid understanding of relational databases such as MS SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, or PostgreSQL
SQL knowledge to query, transform, and validate data across complex datasets
Experience in data modeling and designing mapping sheets, including understanding data lineage (source → transformation → consumption)
Ability to work in domain-oriented architectures (source and consumption domains, data products) with a “shift-left” mindset close to the data source
Analytical mindset with the ability to understand business logic, ensure data consistency, and translate requirements into structured data flows
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as Azure, Google, GCP or AWS
Comfortable working in an agile development environment (e.g., Scrum, Kanban)




Requirements - Nice to have

Experience in Denodo
Experience in building ETL pipelines (Extract, Transform, Load)
Proficiency in Python for data manipulation or automation
Strong proficiency in Data Analytics and Data Visualization principles. 3+ years of hands-on experience with at least one modern reporting tool.
Proven skills in designing and developing interactive dashboards and reports
Exposure to Microsoft Fabric and Power BI environments




Languages

English – B2 / C1




What we offer

Hybrid work model and flexible working schedule that would suit night owls and early birds
Excellent compensation package
25 holiday days per year
Free English classes
Possibilities of career development and the opportunity to shape the company future
An employee-centric culture directly inspired by employee feedback - your voice is heard, and your perspective encouraged
Different training programs to support your personal and professional development
Work in a fast growing, international company
Friendly atmosphere and supportive Management team

Show more

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
About Cigna Healthcare

Cigna Healthcare is a global health service company dedicated to transforming healthcare. With roots in the U.S. and operations in over 30 countries, we serve more than 180 million customers and patients worldwide. Ranked 13th on the Fortune 500 in 2025, Cigna is recognized as one of the most trusted and influential names in the industry.

Our mission is to improve the health, well-being, and peace of mind of those we serve.

Join our globally recognized brand, where trust, communication, and a positive culture are at the core of everything we do. Our leadership is consistent, approachable, and supportive-ensuring your well-being and work-life balance.

We're looking for individuals who thrive in collaborative environments, are passionate about meaningful change, and want to grow in a company that puts people first.

At Cigna, you'll be part of a purpose-driven team that values innovation, compassion, and impact. Whether you're shaping better care experiences or supporting customers through life's key moments, your work will matter.

Grow with us-and help shape the future of healthcare

About The Role

We are seeking a motivated and business-savvy team member to join our Data Science team in a hybrid role that bridges deep domain expertise with analytical growth. This position is ideal for someone with strong knowledge of our health insurance business—especially claims processing area—and a genuine interest in evolving toward data analytics.

You will work closely with experienced data scientists and analysts, contributing to impactful projects that improve our understanding of claims data, claims patterns, operational efficiency and payment integrity activity. With support from the team and access to advanced tools, you’ll progressively build your analytical skills and grow into a full-fledged data analyst.

Key Responsibilities


Act as a domain expert in projects involving claims, member and provider behaviours, operational processes and payment integrity strategy.
Translate business needs into data questions and support the framing of analytical problems.
Collaborate with data scientists to validate data relevance and interpret model outputs.
Assist in preparing datasets for stakeholder needs.
Build basic queries for relevant data extractions.
Learn and apply basic data analysis techniques under mentorship.
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and performance monitoring.


What We’re Looking For


Strong understanding of our expat health insurance business.
Experience in claims processing-related roles, with a track record of excellent performance.
Critical thinking skills and a structured approach to problem-solving.
Curiosity and motivation to learn data tools and analytics.
Clear communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally.
Basic familiarity with Excel, dashboards, or reporting tools is a plus.
Comfort with basic mathematics and statistics is a plus.
No coding experience required, but willingness to learn is essential.


Why Join Us?


Be part of a high-performing, collaborative data science team.
Learn from experienced professionals and cutting-edge tools.
Shape the future of data-driven decision-making in our business.
Grow your career in analytics while staying close to the business.


About Cigna Healthcare

Cigna Healthcare, a division of The Cigna Group, is an advocate for better health through every stage of life. We guide our customers through the health care system, empowering them with the information and insight they need to make the best choices for improving their health and vitality. Join us in driving growth and improving lives.

Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, age, disability, sex, childbirth (including pregnancy) or related medical conditions including but not limited to lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran or military status, religion, national origin, ancestry, marital or familial status, genetic information, status with regard to public assistance, citizenship status or any other characteristic protected by applicable equal employment opportunity laws.

If you require reasonable accommodation in completing the online application process, please email: SeeYourselfEMEA@cigna.com for support. Do not email SeeYourselfEMEA@cigna.com for an update on your application or to provide your resume as you will not receive a response.

Title: Data Analyst (H/M/X)
Company: Grupo de Seguros El Corte Inglés
Description:
Tras la alianza entre Mutua Madrileña y El Corte Inglés, nos encontramos ante un proyecto único en el sector asegurador. Un ambicioso plan de crecimiento y de futuro que aglutina innovación con tradición, ya que, ambos grupos empresariales comparten valores como la excelencia en el servicio, la lealtad a sus clientes y el compromiso con sus empleados.




Si quieres formar parte de este apasionante proyecto, el momento es ahora.




Work style:

Nuestras modernas oficinas centrales se encuentran en pleno centro de Madrid, en la calle Princesa 47. Un espacio de trabajo de estilo industrial que cumple estándares de accesibilidad física y sensorial y que, además, está concienciado con el medio ambiente (en nuestras oficinas “sin papeles”, contamos también con diversos puntos de reciclaje y gestión de residuos).




¿Cuál sería tu rol?

Serás Data Analyst dentro del área de Operación Comercial, una pieza clave para entender cómo funcionan los equipos de venta y ayudar a maximizar su rendimiento.




Trabajarás muy cerca del negocio, analizando presupuestos, evaluando resultados y detectando oportunidades de mejora para impulsar la eficiencia comercial.




👉 En otras palabras: convertirás datos en decisiones que mejoran la performance de ventas.




Funciones:

Participarás en el análisis y estimación de presupuestos comerciales, asegurando un seguimiento continuo del cumplimiento de objetivos.
Darás soporte a la dinamización de los canales de venta, analizando indicadores clave y proponiendo acciones que mejoren su rendimiento.
Trabajarás en el análisis de la retribución variable, ayudando a asegurar que esté alineada con los resultados del negocio y con el desempeño de los equipos.
Contribuirás a la elaboración de informes de rendimiento y a la identificación de oportunidades para optimizar la gestión y eficiencia de la fuerza de ventas.




¿Qué estamos buscando?

Buscamos a alguien con mentalidad analítica pero también muy conectado al negocio, capaz de entender qué hay detrás de los números y cómo influyen en el rendimiento comercial.




Valoramos especialmente la proactividad y la actitud: alguien que se anticipe, que proponga mejoras y que no se quede solo en el análisis, sino que aporte soluciones.




También es clave que seas una persona colaborativa, que sume al equipo y que disfrute trabajando de forma cercana con otros, compartiendo conocimientos y contribuyendo a un buen ambiente.




Buscamos a alguien implicado, con atención al detalle, capacidad resolutiva y una comunicación clara y positiva, capaz de trasladar insights de forma sencilla y útil para el negocio.




Requisitos:

Grado en ADE, Economía, Estadística o similar.
Experiencia previa en análisis comercial, ventas o roles similares.
Nivel avanzado de Excel y Power BI
Valorable conocimiento en SQL y Python.
Alta capacidad analítica, atención al detalle y orientación al trabajo en equipo.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación, tanto escritas como orales.




¿Qué vas a encontrar aquí?

Aquí encontrarás un entorno muy conectado al negocio, donde tu trabajo tendrá un impacto directo en cómo funcionan y evolucionan los equipos de venta. No estarás solo en el dato: entenderás el contexto y verás cómo tus análisis se traducen en decisiones reales.




Formarás parte de un equipo cercano y con experiencia, donde la colaboración, el apoyo y el buen ambiente son clave en el día a día. Además, contarás con un liderazgo accesible que valora la implicación, la confianza y el trabajo bien hecho.




Tendrás la oportunidad de aprender de la operativa comercial desde dentro, enfrentándote a retos reales y participando en la mejora continua de procesos y resultados.




En definitiva, un entorno donde crecer, aportar y ver el impacto de tu trabajo desde el primer momento.




¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?

HTML:
Tras la alianza entre Mutua Madrileña y El Corte Inglés, nos encontramos ante un proyecto único en el sector asegurador. Un ambicioso plan de crecimiento y de futuro que aglutina innovación con tradición, ya que, ambos grupos empresariales comparten valores como la excelencia en el servicio, la lealtad a sus clientes y el compromiso con sus empleados.


Si quieres formar parte de este apasionante proyecto, el momento es ahora.


Work style:

Nuestras modernas oficinas centrales se encuentran en pleno centro de Madrid, en la calle Princesa 47. Un espacio de trabajo de estilo industrial que cumple estándares de accesibilidad física y sensorial y que, además, está concienciado con el medio ambiente (en nuestras oficinas “sin papeles”, contamos también con diversos puntos de reciclaje y gestión de residuos).


¿Cuál sería tu rol?

Serás Data Analyst dentro del área de Operación Comercial, una pieza clave para entender cómo funcionan los equipos de venta y ayudar a maximizar su rendimiento.


Trabajarás muy cerca del negocio, analizando presupuestos, evaluando resultados y detectando oportunidades de mejora para impulsar la eficiencia comercial.


👉 En otras palabras: convertirás datos en decisiones que mejoran la performance de ventas.


Funciones:

  • Participarás en el análisis y estimación de presupuestos comerciales, asegurando un seguimiento continuo del cumplimiento de objetivos.
  • Darás soporte a la dinamización de los canales de venta, analizando indicadores clave y proponiendo acciones que mejoren su rendimiento.
  • Trabajarás en el análisis de la retribución variable, ayudando a asegurar que esté alineada con los resultados del negocio y con el desempeño de los equipos.
  • Contribuirás a la elaboración de informes de rendimiento y a la identificación de oportunidades para optimizar la gestión y eficiencia de la fuerza de ventas.


¿Qué estamos buscando?

Buscamos a alguien con mentalidad analítica pero también muy conectado al negocio, capaz de entender qué hay detrás de los números y cómo influyen en el rendimiento comercial.


Valoramos especialmente la proactividad y la actitud: alguien que se anticipe, que proponga mejoras y que no se quede solo en el análisis, sino que aporte soluciones.


También es clave que seas una persona colaborativa, que sume al equipo y que disfrute trabajando de forma cercana con otros, compartiendo conocimientos y contribuyendo a un buen ambiente.


Buscamos a alguien implicado, con atención al detalle, capacidad resolutiva y una comunicación clara y positiva, capaz de trasladar insights de forma sencilla y útil para el negocio.


Requisitos:

  • Grado en ADE, Economía, Estadística o similar.
  • Experiencia previa en análisis comercial, ventas o roles similares.
  • Nivel avanzado de Excel y Power BI
  • Valorable conocimiento en SQL y Python.
  • Alta capacidad analítica, atención al detalle y orientación al trabajo en equipo.
  • Buenas habilidades de comunicación, tanto escritas como orales.


¿Qué vas a encontrar aquí?

Aquí encontrarás un entorno muy conectado al negocio, donde tu trabajo tendrá un impacto directo en cómo funcionan y evolucionan los equipos de venta. No estarás solo en el dato: entenderás el contexto y verás cómo tus análisis se traducen en decisiones reales.


Formarás parte de un equipo cercano y con experiencia, donde la colaboración, el apoyo y el buen ambiente son clave en el día a día. Además, contarás con un liderazgo accesible que valora la implicación, la confianza y el trabajo bien hecho.


Tendrás la oportunidad de aprender de la operativa comercial desde dentro, enfrentándote a retos reales y participando en la mejora continua de procesos y resultados.


En definitiva, un entorno donde crecer, aportar y ver el impacto de tu trabajo desde el primer momento.


¿Qué podemos ofrecerte?

  • Contrato laboral indefinido desde el primer día
  • Modelo híbrido de trabajo 30% en remoto

Title: Data Analyst Créditos y Cobros - Data & Finanzas
Description:
🏠🔐Movistar Prosegur Alarmas (MPA), Joint-Venture de Telefónica y Prosegur, es un referente del sector de las alarmas en España que proporciona a hogares, empresas y comercios una seguridad de confianza basada en las soluciones más avanzadas del mercado. MPA cuenta en la actualidad con una base de clientes de 600K, que se ha más que duplicado desde la creación de la JV en el 2020.

¿Y tú? ¿Te sientes identificado con nuestro propósito? ¿Te apasionan los retos y quieres llevar tu carrera profesional a otro nivel? Hoy tienes la oportunidad de hacerlo sumándote a nuestro Team of Teams de Movistar Prosegur Alarmas como Analista de Datos de Créditos y Cobros




🎯 Misión del puesto:

Buscamos una persona con sólida base financiera y experiencia en análisis de datos, que quiera formar parte de un entorno donde el análisis tiene un impacto directo en la toma de decisiones y en la optimización de la gestión de deuda.

La persona seleccionada trabajará con bases de datos de deuda pendiente de clientes, siendo responsable de transformar grandes volúmenes de información financiera en análisis estructurados, segmentaciones útiles y cuadros de mando industrializados.

El rol combina visión financiera, capacidad analítica y orientación a negocio, participando activamente en la definición de indicadores clave y en la comunicación de resultados a distintos niveles de la organización.

Es una excelente oportunidad para aprender, crecer y tener un papel protagonista en la creación y estructuración de un área de análisis y reporting.




🧩 Funciones principales

Análisis y explotación de datos financieros de deuda y facturación: tipología, movimientos mensuales, insolvencias, etc.
Definición e implementación de procedimientos de reporting recurrente para apoyar la toma de decisiones.
Seguimiento de indicadores financieros, detección de desviaciones, patrones y oportunidades de mejora.
Identificación y mejora de procesos automáticos en herramientas de gestión de deuda.
Colaboración con el equipo de BI en la creación de cuadros de mando.
Definición y evolución de KPIs financieros y de cobros.




✅ Requisitos

Grado en ADE o similar.
Valorable Máster en Análisis de Datos / Data Analytics.
Excel avanzado (Power Query) y DAX.
SQL (imprescindible).
Valorable conocimiento de Python (Pandas).
Experiencia o interés en gestión de deuda, cobros, facturación o contabilidad clientes.
Se valorará experiencia con herramientas de gestión de cobros.
Conocimiento (aunque sea básico) de ERP Cloud (Oracle), Salesforce o Siebel.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación y trabajo transversal.




⭐ Se ofrece

Proyecto con impacto real en negocio.
Entorno colaborativo y orientado a datos.
Desarrollo profesional en un área estratégica.
Condiciones competitivas acorde a experiencia.
Salario fijo + variable por consecución de objetivos.




👉 ¿Te interesa? Inscríbete y da el siguiente paso en tu carrera como analista de datos financiero data-driven.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
🔹 Opportunity – Business Data Analyst 🔹




We are looking for a Business Data Analyst to help assume full responsibility for the development and optimization of dashboards and reporting systems, ensuring high-quality data while delivering strategic insights to enhance product presentation, analysis, and sales in a competitive digital environment.




📌 About the role

You will integrate into a team where specialization in reporting and dashboard development is essential for driving strategic decision-making and operational optimization.

You will take complete ownership of the reporting function—designing, developing, and optimizing interactive dashboards that visualize critical KPIs. Far from being a purely technical role, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to translate complex data sets into actionable business insights, support UX research initiatives, and assist with A/B testing analysis to optimize online store features, product placements, and promotional strategies across desktop and mobile applications.




What we’re looking for:

Experience: +4 years of experience in an analytical role, with a strong mindset oriented toward business impact and decision-making.
Technical Background: Proficient with data analytics and reporting tools such as Excel, SQL, and Google Analytics, alongside advanced skills in developing interactive dashboards (such as Tableau) to visualize KPIs.
Optimization Focus: Solid experience or exposure to UX research and A/B testing analysis within an online sales, e-commerce, or digital product context.
Stakeholder & Project Management: Ability to prioritize requirements from various stakeholders, work with cross-functional teams to gather market data, and ensure data quality and reliability.




📍 Location: Hybrid Model - Barcelona

Sector: Digital E-Commerce / Ancillary Product




Why join this project?




🤝 People first – inclusive, diverse, and international culture.

🚀 Work with cutting-edge technology on global-scale products.

📈 Clear opportunities for career progression and continuous development.

😁 Collaborative teams with a strong sense of belonging.

🎓 Access to learning resources and development programs.

💰 Competitive compensation.

🩺 Private health insurance & benefits (according to region).

⚡ Flexible working environment and hybrid model.

🏋️ Wellness and mental-health initiatives.

🥳 Team-building activities and global community events.




✨ Want to know more about us? Click here and discover all the details.

🔍 Find out what people are saying about us on Glassdoor Reviews.







💬 We know that not every candidate will meet 100% of the requirements. If your profile doesn’t match perfectly but you believe you can add value, we would still love to hear from you!




👉 Ready for the challenge? Apply now and be part of a global digital leader driving innovation and transformation.




Empowering People, Unlocking Innovation.




The employee will adhere to information security policies:

-Will have access to confidential information related to Capitole and the project they are working on.

-Must comply with the security policies and internal policies of the company and the client.

-Must sign an NDA.

Title: Data Analyst Looker
Description:
¡Hola! 🙋




¿Todavía no conoces hiberus? Somos una empresa de #tecnología construida con un ingrediente diferencial, la HIPERESPECIALIZACIÓN.




Formar parte de hiberus significa crecimiento, pasión por la tecnología, interés por la innovación, ambiente laboral flexible y colaborativo, compañerismo, aprendizaje, formación continua, motivación y superación ante nuevos retos... y esto es solo el principio.




Contamos con un equipo de más de 4.500 profesionales, 46 áreas de especialización y 35 hubs de desarrollo en España, Europa, Latinoamérica, Estados Unidos y África. Somos la primera empresa tecnológica española de capital privado. En los últimos cuatro años, hemos experimentado un crecimiento exponencial, duplicando nuestro volumen de negocio año tras año. Esto nos ha permitido consolidarnos como un actor clave en el ecosistema tecnológico español y mantenernos competitivos frente a los líderes internacionales ¡Estamos redefiniendo el mapa tecnológico mundial! 🌐💻




Trabajamos muy duro, para transformar nuestra vida a través de la tecnología y queremos contar contigo para seguir creciendo. Si lo tuyo es el mundo IT, te gusta estar al día en las últimas tecnologías y quieres asumir un nuevo reto profesional ¡Sigue leyendo, te estamos buscando!




🔎¿Qué estamos buscando?

Buscamos un/a Data Analyst con experiencia y conocimientos en Looker, capaz de transformar datos en insights de valor para negocio. Queremos una persona con mentalidad analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para trabajar con diferentes stakeholders, aportando valor real a través del dato.

Buscamos una persona que nos ayude a seguir creciendo, con ganas de aportar y seguir evolucionando de la mano de los mejores profesionales, pero, sobre todo,... ¡en hiberus buscamos buena gente!😉




💻Algunas de tus funciones

Diseñar, desarrollar y mantener dashboards e informes en Looker.
Analizar datos y generar insights accionables para negocio.
Definir métricas y KPIs junto a equipos de negocio y tecnología.
Trabajar con modelos de datos y asegurar su correcta explotación.
Optimizar consultas y procesos de análisis de datos.
Colaborar con equipos de Data Engineering y BI para mejorar la calidad y disponibilidad del dato.




💻💪🏻Habilidades y requisitos

Experiencia previa como Data Analyst (2-4 años aprox.).
Experiencia trabajando con Looker / Looker Studio.
Conocimiento sólido de SQL.
Experiencia trabajando con grandes volúmenes de datos.
Capacidad analítica y orientación a negocio.
Experiencia en definición de KPIs y reporting.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación.
Capacidad de trabajo en equipo y autonomía.

⭐ Se valorará

Experiencia en entornos cloud (GCP, AWS o Azure).
Conocimientos en modelado de datos.
Experiencia en otras herramientas BI (Power BI, Tableau…).
Conocimientos básicos de Python o herramientas de análisis.
Experiencia en entornos ágiles.




🙌¿Qué te ofrecemos?

💼 Contrato indefinido en una compañía puramente tecnológica, que forma parte de un gran grupo, solvente y en crecimiento.

💰 Salario fijo competitivo

🏖️ Ambiente familiar, cercano, ¡como una gran familia!

⏰ Conciliación con nuestra vida personal y laboral mediante horario flexible, desconexión digital, jornada intensiva viernes y verano.

🤙 Cultura “techie”, nos gusta estar en contacto con la tecnología, herramientas, y últimas novedades!

📚 ¡Formación! Siempre que quieras, disfrutarás de un amplio catálogo de cursos formativos, adaptados a tu perfil profesional, inquietudes y novedades del sector. Para ello, pondremos a tu disposición todo el potencial de nuestra Hiberus University y los acuerdos con los principales fabricantes.

💳 ¡Benefíciate! Programa de retribución flexible a medida: seguro médico, tarjeta de transporte público, cheques guardería, tarjeta restaurante, etc.




En hiberus estamos viviendo un crecimiento explosivo 💥 y queremos que formes parte de nuestro equipo.




Suena bien, ¿verdad? Si quieres saber más, ¡inscríbete y te contamos!




Si quieres saber más busca nuestros hashtag

#somoshiberus #lascosasocurrenaquí y conoce todo lo que hacemos.




Hablamos?




www.hiberus.com

#somosHiberus y ¿tú? ¿Te animas?🚀

Title: Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics
Description:
🔹 Senior Data Analyst | Data & Insights | AI-Driven Analytics 🔹







📌 About the role




We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join a Data & Insights team within an international transformation environment.

In this role, you will play a key part in shaping how data supports decision-making across the organisation, working at the intersection of advanced analytics, business performance, and emerging AI capabilities.

You will contribute to high-impact initiatives across commercial performance, operational analytics, finance insights, and the evolution of self-service analytics and data products.

This is a highly business-facing role, ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex datasets into clear insights, building meaningful KPIs, supporting senior stakeholders, and exploring how AI can improve productivity, insight generation, and data discoverability.

If you enjoy working with SQL, Python, Power BI, large datasets, business stakeholders, and AI-driven analytics tools, this could be a great fit.







💻 What you’ll do




🔹 Deliver high-quality analytical insights to support strategic decision-making across commercial, operational, and finance domains

🔹 Translate complex data into clear, structured, and actionable outputs for business and senior stakeholders

🔹 Prepare reports, dashboards, and performance analysis suitable for senior management and executive-level decision-making

🔹 Work closely with business stakeholders to understand needs, define relevant KPIs, and build performance frameworks

🔹 Analyse business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational efficiency, customer behaviour, and commercial performance

🔹 Support and enhance existing reporting solutions, including Power BI dashboards and performance reports

🔹 Contribute to the design and evolution of self-service analytics solutions and data products

🔹 Collaborate with Data Engineers and platform teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability

🔹 Work with large datasets using SQL, Python, Power BI, and other analytical tools

🔹 Identify trends, patterns, risks, and opportunities through data analysis and business context

🔹 Explore and experiment with AI-driven tools and approaches to improve insight generation, productivity, and data accessibility

🔹 Help improve data adoption across the organisation by making insights clear, accessible, and actionable

🔹 Communicate complex analytical topics in a clear and structured way to both technical and non-technical audiences

🔹 Work in a fast-paced, transformation-driven environment with cross-functional teams







💡 Must Have




🔹 6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data & Insights, or similar roles

🔹 Strong experience working with large datasets and analytical tools

🔹 Strong proficiency in SQL

🔹 Experience with Python for data analysis, automation, or analytical workflows

🔹 Strong experience with Power BI or similar BI / data visualisation tools

🔹 Proven ability to turn complex data into actionable business insights

🔹 Experience building dashboards, reports, KPIs, and performance analysis for business users

🔹 Good understanding of business performance metrics such as revenue, cost, operational KPIs, commercial performance, or finance metrics

🔹 Experience working with stakeholders at different levels, including senior business stakeholders

🔹 Ability to communicate complex topics in a clear, structured, and business-oriented way

🔹 Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and attention to detail

🔹 Curiosity and willingness to experiment with new technologies, especially AI-driven analytics tools

🔹 Comfortable working in ambiguous, fast-moving, and transformation-driven environments

🔹 Intermediate English level or above







✨ Nice to Have




🔹 Experience preparing reporting for senior management, executive committees, or management committees

🔹 Experience contributing to self-service analytics, data products, or reusable reporting frameworks

🔹 Exposure to AI tools and technologies such as Generative AI, analytics copilots, ML tools, ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, or similar

🔹 Experience using AI tools to improve productivity, automate analysis, generate insights, or support data discoverability

🔹 Experience collaborating with Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers, or platform teams

🔹 Experience with data quality, data governance, data modelling, or analytics engineering practices

🔹 Experience with Tableau, Qlik, Looker, Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, or similar tools

🔹 Background in commercial analytics, operational analytics, finance analytics, pricing, revenue management, or performance tracking







🏢 Hybrid model - 2 days on site per week







🌍 Why join this project?




🤝 People first – diverse and inclusive culture in an international environment.

📊 Work on high-impact Data & Insights initiatives across business performance, operations, finance, and commercial domains.

🚀 Help shape how data supports strategic decision-making across a large international organisation.

🧠 Explore the evolution of AI-driven analytics, productivity tools, and data discoverability.

⚙️ Contribute to self-service analytics, data products, and scalable reporting practices.

📈 Work closely with business and senior stakeholders, turning complex data into meaningful insights.

😁 High team stability and collaborative culture.

🎓 €1200 per year training budget and continuous learning opportunities.

💰 Flexible compensation model.

🩺 Private health insurance and benefits package.

⚡ Flexible working hours and hybrid model.

🏋️ Wellhub: fitness, wellness, and mental health support.

⚽ Football and paddle tennis teams sponsored by Capitole.

🥳 Team buildings, global events, and strong tech communities.

🔍 Curious about our culture? Check out what people are saying about us on Glassdoor.




💬 We know that not every candidate will meet 100% of the requirements. If your profile doesn’t match perfectly but you believe you can add value, we’d still love to hear from you.




👉 Ready for the challenge? Apply now!




Empowering People, Unlocking Innovation.

The employee will have access to confidential information related to Capitole and the assigned project.
Compliance with internal security and information protection policies is mandatory.
NDA signature required.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Pozuelo de Alarcón, Community of Madrid, Spain
Description:
Shape the future of security with data

At Verisure, we believe security is a human right. We are a global leader in professionally monitored security solutions, combining scale with a fast-moving, entrepreneurial mindset.

We’re now looking for a Data Analyst to join one of our most strategic areas: Proposition Management, working at the intersection of technology, data, and business. Please note this is a temporary position to cover a long-term medical leave, with the duration flexible depending on the employee’s return.

Your mission

This role sits within our Proposition team. This area ensures we have a strong pipeline of innovative products and services and defines new products and services for the company.

In Practice, You Will


Define and track KPIs for new products and services from early stages
Partner with Product, BI & Tech teams to build data models and dashboards
Monitor product performance and lead KPI follow-ups
Deliver deep-dive analysis to support strategic product decisions
Transform large datasets into actionable insights and recommendations
Support business cases and scenario analysis for growth initiatives
Drive reporting and presentations for senior stakeholders


In this role, you will operate with a high level of autonomy and collaborate with stakeholders across the entire organization — from Product and Business to Technology and BI.

Why this role stands out


Direct exposure to senior leadership — including regular interaction with the CTO
Unique position between data and business, with clear visibility on how insights drive decisions
Strong strategic perspective on where the company is heading
Opportunity to influence the full product lifecycle — from ideation to performance tracking


What We’re Looking For


Solid experience in data analysis, reporting, and data modelling
Strong knowledge of SQL, Power BI, and data visualisation
Ability to work hands-on with data and translate it into business insights
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present to senior stakeholders
Structured but able to see the bigger strategic picture
Entrepreneurial and committed, with a strong hands-on approach
Good understanding and passion about technology
Proficiency in English; other languages are a plus.


About Verisure

At Verisure, we do something amazing every day. By protecting what matters most, we change lives for the better. We are the leading provider of professionally monitored security services with 24/7 response in Europe and Latin America. More than 6 million families and small businesses in 18 countries rely on our innovative technology to protect what matters most to them.

We integrate product development, design, sales, installation services and a 24/7 professional monitoring solution. We have a track record of continuous growth, as well as exciting plans to continue expanding, transforming, and leading the market into the future.

Our success depends on our people, and we invest in them every day. Working with Verisure is being part of an industry-leading, world-class company that has a strong entrepreneurial spirit.

Our Company DNA


Passionate in Everything We Do: Our people have a sense of energy that is unmistakable, one that drives us to delight our customers and focus on creating impact quickly.
Committed to Making a Difference: When we say we will do something; we deliver with excellence. We are accountable, focused and operate with discipline.
Always Innovating: We believe that Innovation can be big or small; it’s a continuous state of mind that inspires us to think differently and always make things better.
Winning as a Team: Our people know that by leveraging one another’s strengths, investing in and developing our team’s capability and by collaborating well, we will win.
With Trust & Responsibility: Operating with integrity is core to our success. We are humble, honest and value deep mastery and expertise. We do the right thing, always.


Diversity, inclusion, equity, and belonging at Verisure

At Verisure, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace, recognising that diversity of thought and background only makes our teams stronger and more innovative. We reject all forms of discrimination and bias, and we believe in access to opportunities for everyone, regardless of gender, age, disability status, race, sexual orientation, or any other status.

Apply today!

We are continuously reviewing candidates, so we encourage you to apply as soon as you can. If you have questions regarding this position, please reach out to our Group TA Specialist, Elvira Torres at Elvira.torres.benavente@verisure.com

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Buscamos Data Analyst | Vallés Oriental




¿Te apasiona convertir datos en decisiones estratégicas? En una compañía en crecimiento del sector distribución, buscamos incorporar un/a Data Analyst que quiera tener impacto real en el negocio.




¿Cuál será tu misión?

Transformar datos en insights accionables que impulsen el crecimiento, optimicen la rentabilidad y ayuden a tomar mejores decisiones en diferentes áreas del negocio.




¿Qué harás en tu día a día?

Analizar el comportamiento de clientes y productos para identificar oportunidades de mejora.
Monitorizar KPIs y crear dashboards claros y útiles para negocio.
Detectar tendencias, riesgos y oportunidades de crecimiento.
Apoyar decisiones de pricing y promociones mediante análisis de datos.
Colaborar con equipos de Sales, Marketing, Finanzas y Supply Chain.
Traducir datos complejos en insights comprensibles para perfiles no técnicos.




Qué buscamos

2-5 años de experiencia en análisis de datos.
Formación en Data, ADE, Economía, Ingeniería o similar.
Nivel avanzado de Excel y experiencia en herramientas BI (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
Conocimientos de SQL y manejo de grandes volúmenes de datos.
Perfil analítico, orientado al detalle y con visión de negocio.
Buen nivel de inglés.




Qué ofrecemos

Rol estratégico con impacto directo en decisiones de negocio.
Entorno colaborativo y transversal.
Oportunidad de crecimiento profesional en un equipo dinámico.
Ubicación: Vallés Oriental.




Si te interesa o quieres saber más, ¡manda tu CV!

Title: Data Analyst & Power BI Developer
Description:
Objective:




The Data Analyst & Power BI Developer is responsible for transforming business data into strategic insights through advanced analytics, dashboard development, and business intelligence solutions. This role will lead the design, development, and maintenance of Power BI reporting environments, ensuring accurate, timely, and actionable information to support operational, financial, commercial, and guest experience decision-making at SHA.




Main Duties & Responsibilities:




Data Management & Integration:

Extract, integrate, and transform data from multiple internal and external information sources, ensuring consistency, reliability, and accessibility.
Lead the implementation, administration, and optimization of the Data Lake, ensuring an efficient and scalable structure aligned with business needs.
Ensure data quality, integrity, accuracy, and consistency through validation, cleansing, and continuous monitoring processes.
Develop and manage data models to optimize organizational data exploitation and accessibility.




Business Intelligence & Power BI Development:

Design, develop, and implement Power BI dashboards, executive reports, and business scorecards for senior leadership and business units.
Transform complex data into clear, dynamic, and actionable visualizations to support decision-making.
Build and maintain KPIs, business metrics, and analytical models aligned with organizational objectives.
Automate reporting processes and recurring data deliverables to improve operational efficiency.




Business Analytics Support:

Partner with business units to conceptualize, structure, and define analytical and reporting requirements.
Translate operational and strategic business needs into data visualization and analytical solutions.
Identify trends, deviations, risks, opportunities, and actionable insights through data analysis.
Collaborate with department leaders to enhance reporting capabilities and support continuous improvement initiatives.




Data Architecture, Security & Governance:

Implement and maintain the data analytics architecture, ensuring scalability, efficiency, and accessibility of business information.
Define standards and best practices for data modeling, reporting, and data consumption.
Ensure compliance with all Information Security and Data Governance policies, standards, and procedures.
Promote responsible, secure, and compliant data usage across the organization.




Job Profile:




Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology or a related field.
Minimum 2–3 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or analytical solution development.
Proven experience in Power BI development, executive dashboards, and reporting automation.
Experience in data integration, transformation, and modeling across multiple systems and data sources.
Previous experience in luxury hospitality, wellness, real estate, or premium service environments is highly desirable.




Technical Skills

Advanced Power BI (DAX, Power Query, Data Modeling)
SQL and relational databases
Data Lake architecture and management
ETL Processes (Extract, Transform & Load)
Advanced Excel
KPI development and executive reporting
Data Quality & Data Governance
Qlikview
Python
Azure and AI.

Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
Tech Pipeline helps operations teams fix the friction in their business processes by focusing on technical reality instead of abstract strategy. We are currently working with a financial services partner in the Mediterranean to address gaps in their reporting pipelines and data routing. This Graduate Data Analyst position is a full time and fully remote role centered on making sure numbers stop drifting and systems stay reconciled.

About the role
Many companies struggle when their tech stack grows faster than their internal processes. In this role, you will help us bridge the gap between their current tools and what actually needs to happen for the business to function. You will not be building high level decks. Instead, you will spend your time looking at why approval chains might be stuck or why two databases are showing different totals. By identifying the specific routing rule or integration gap that is causing trouble, you help us hand over a system that the client can eventually run on their own. This position is based entirely remote, allowing you to manage your tasks from your own workspace while staying closely aligned with our technical team.

What you will be doing
Investigate logs to find where data entries are getting stuck or failing to sync correctly.
Perform monthly reconciliations between different financial systems to identify discrepancies.
Document the current state of routing rules and suggest updates to clear operational backlogs.
Trace the lifecycle of a single transaction through the entire pipeline to spot points of friction.
Update standard operating procedures so the client team knows how to manage the fix independently.
Assist in building reporting pipelines that provide a clear source of truth for the finance team.
Check that manual data entry points are replaced with reliable automated routing where possible.
Collaborate with the technical team to test new configurations before they go live in the production environment.



What we need from you
A degree in a quantitative field like Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related subject.
A basic understanding of SQL for querying databases and pulling relevant information.
Intermediate skills in Excel, including the ability to use lookups and pivot tables for data audit.
A clear writing style that can explain technical problems to non-technical operators.
Strong attention to detail and a preference for accuracy over speed in financial reporting.
The ability to work independently in a fully remote environment without constant supervision.
A logical approach to troubleshooting where you follow the evidence rather than making guesses.
Curiosity about how business operations actually function under the hood of a large organisation.



Helpful background
Previous experience with a student project or internship involving financial data or accounting.
General exposure to ERP systems or common business software suites used in office environments.
Basic knowledge of a scripting language like Python for automating small, repetitive tasks.



What the role offers
A permanent, full time position with a focus on professional growth and practical skill building.
A competitive salary that is attractive for the current graduate market and reflects the value you bring.
The flexibility of a fully remote work arrangement, allowing you to work from wherever you are comfortable.
Direct mentorship from senior technical leads who value practical, hands-on work over theory.
The chance to work on tangible problems where your fixes are put into production within weeks.
Exposure to the operational realities and data structures of a large Mediterranean financial services firm.
A work culture that prizes documentation and clarity over endless meetings.



Working at Tech Pipeline
We believe that quality work happens when people have the time to concentrate on specific, technical problems. Our internal communication reflects this: we rely heavily on weekly written updates and thorough documentation rather than spending all day in video calls. As a remote team, we stay connected through clear expectations and a shared focus on ground truth. We value people who like to see how things work and who feel a sense of satisfaction when a messy backlog is finally cleared.

HTML:
Tech Pipeline helps operations teams fix the friction in their business processes by focusing on technical reality instead of abstract strategy. We are currently working with a financial services partner in the Mediterranean to address gaps in their reporting pipelines and data routing. This Graduate Data Analyst position is a full time and fully remote role centered on making sure numbers stop drifting and systems stay reconciled.

About the role
 Many companies struggle when their tech stack grows faster than their internal processes. In this role, you will help us bridge the gap between their current tools and what actually needs to happen for the business to function. You will not be building high level decks. Instead, you will spend your time looking at why approval chains might be stuck or why two databases are showing different totals. By identifying the specific routing rule or integration gap that is causing trouble, you help us hand over a system that the client can eventually run on their own. This position is based entirely remote, allowing you to manage your tasks from your own workspace while staying closely aligned with our technical team.

What you will be doing
  • Investigate logs to find where data entries are getting stuck or failing to sync correctly.
  • Perform monthly reconciliations between different financial systems to identify discrepancies.
  • Document the current state of routing rules and suggest updates to clear operational backlogs.
  • Trace the lifecycle of a single transaction through the entire pipeline to spot points of friction.
  • Update standard operating procedures so the client team knows how to manage the fix independently.
  • Assist in building reporting pipelines that provide a clear source of truth for the finance team.
  • Check that manual data entry points are replaced with reliable automated routing where possible.
  • Collaborate with the technical team to test new configurations before they go live in the production environment.


What we need from you
  • A degree in a quantitative field like Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related subject.
  • A basic understanding of SQL for querying databases and pulling relevant information.
  • Intermediate skills in Excel, including the ability to use lookups and pivot tables for data audit.
  • A clear writing style that can explain technical problems to non-technical operators.
  • Strong attention to detail and a preference for accuracy over speed in financial reporting.
  • The ability to work independently in a fully remote environment without constant supervision.
  • A logical approach to troubleshooting where you follow the evidence rather than making guesses.
  • Curiosity about how business operations actually function under the hood of a large organisation.


Helpful background
  • Previous experience with a student project or internship involving financial data or accounting.
  • General exposure to ERP systems or common business software suites used in office environments.
  • Basic knowledge of a scripting language like Python for automating small, repetitive tasks.


What the role offers
  • A permanent, full time position with a focus on professional growth and practical skill building.
  • A competitive salary that is attractive for the current graduate market and reflects the value you bring.
  • The flexibility of a fully remote work arrangement, allowing you to work from wherever you are comfortable.
  • Direct mentorship from senior technical leads who value practical, hands-on work over theory.
  • The chance to work on tangible problems where your fixes are put into production within weeks.
  • Exposure to the operational realities and data structures of a large Mediterranean financial services firm.
  • A work culture that prizes documentation and clarity over endless meetings.


Working at Tech Pipeline
 We believe that quality work happens when people have the time to concentrate on specific, technical problems. Our internal communication reflects this: we rely heavily on weekly written updates and thorough documentation rather than spending all day in video calls. When we join a project, we are there to solve the problem and then step back once the client is ready to take the reins. As a remote team, we stay connected through clear expectations and a shared focus on ground truth. We want our systems to be so clear that there is no need for a manual or a long term retainer once our work is done. We value people who like to see how things work and who feel a sense of satisfaction when a messy backlog is finally cleared.

Title: Junior BI Analyst
Description:
Bavarian Capital is expanding its analytical capabilities to support a long term commitment to a major Mediterranean client. We are looking for a Junior BI Analyst to help us manage data assets and provide clear, reliable reporting for this significant partnership. This role is fundamental to how we monitor performance and maintain the high standards of accuracy our partners expect from us.

About the role
The Junior BI Analyst will work as part of our data team to translate raw information into useful insights. In this position, you will help bridge the gap between complex datasets and the practical needs of our investment professionals. The role is fully remote, allowing you to work from your own environment while remaining closely connected to our distributed team through regular communication and structured workflows. You will focus on maintaining the integrity of our data pipelines and ensuring that all reporting reflects the current state of our investments with precision.

What you will do
Collect and organize data from various sources to support our Mediterranean client engagement.
Build and maintain basic dashboards using standard business intelligence tools to track performance metrics.
Perform regular data quality checks to ensure the accuracy of all reporting and identify potential errors.
Assist in the preparation of weekly and monthly summaries for internal stakeholders and external partners.
Identify patterns or anomalies in datasets and flag them for further investigation by the senior team.
Collaborate with other analysts to refine data collection processes and improve reporting efficiency.
Document data sources and report definitions to maintain clear internal records and knowledge sharing.
Support the team with ad hoc data requests and basic statistical analysis as they arise.



What we are looking for
A foundational understanding of data analysis principles and a desire to learn new methodologies.
Basic proficiency in SQL for querying and extracting information from relational databases.
Strong skills in spreadsheet software, particularly in using formulas, functions, and pivot tables to organize data.
Familiarity with business intelligence software such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
A clear and logical approach to solving problems and organizing complex information.
Good written and verbal communication skills for explaining technical details to non technical team members.
The ability to manage individual tasks effectively and meet deadlines in a remote work setting.
An interest in financial markets, capital management, and long term investment strategies.
A degree or recent coursework in a quantitative field such as mathematics, economics, or computer science.



What we offer
A fully remote working arrangement that prioritizes professional autonomy and work life balance.
A paid position with a competitive compensation package that is attractive for the current market.
The opportunity to work on a high profile project for a major Mediterranean client.
A calm and professional work environment that values deep focus and high quality output.
Access to experienced mentors who take the time to support your professional growth.
Standardized processes that provide clear guidance on goals and daily expectations.
Support for ongoing learning and the further development of technical skills in data analytics.
A stable, long term career path within an established investment firm that avoids short term trends.



Working at Bavarian Capital
Bavarian Capital operates on the belief that a stable environment produces the best results. We do not subscribe to the frantic pace often found in the technology or finance sectors: instead, we prioritize steady progress and thoughtful analysis. Our firm is built on a foundation of mutual respect and professional independence. As a remote first company, we trust our team members to handle their responsibilities with diligence and care without the need for constant supervision. We value clarity in communication and a straightforward approach to every task. By working with us, you join a team that values your craft and provides the space you need to produce your best work while contributing to the sustainable growth of our clients.

Title: DPO Analista de Datos
Place: Villanueva de la Cañada, Community of Madrid, Spain
Description:
La Universidad Camilo José Cela busca incorporar a un/a Analista de Datos.

Funciones


Analizar datos de negocio para identificar tendencias, desviaciones, oportunidades y áreas de mejora.
Extraer, depurar, transformar y estructurar datos procedentes de distintas fuentes internas y externas.
Diseñar, elaborar, actualizar y mantener informes, dashboards y cuadros de mando para el seguimiento de KPIs.
Interpretar resultados y convertir los datos en recomendaciones útiles para la toma de decisiones.
Dar soporte analítico a los equipos de marketing y promoción en el seguimiento de campañas, acciones comerciales, rendimiento de iniciativas y evolución de indicadores.
Definir, revisar y dar seguimiento a métricas clave del negocio junto con las áreas implicadas.
Realizar análisis recurrentes y ad hoc para responder a necesidades concretas del negocio.
Colaborar con equipos de marketing y promoción para entender necesidades, priorizar requerimientos y traducirlos en soluciones analíticas.
Trabajar transversalmente con TIC / Tecnología, Gestión de Datos y otros departamentos para asegurar la disponibilidad, calidad, integración y correcta explotación de la información.
Asegurar la consistencia, fiabilidad y calidad de los datos utilizados en el reporting y en los análisis.
Identificar oportunidades de automatización de procesos de análisis y reporting para mejorar la eficiencia operativa.
Apoyar la evolución de la cultura de datos en la organización, facilitando una visión más analítica y orientada a evidencia dentro del negocio.
Colaborar en la mejora de procesos, herramientas y metodologías relacionadas con el uso del dato.
Preparar presentaciones e informes ejecutivos para compartir conclusiones con áreas de negocio y otros interlocutores internos.


Requisitos


Formación universitaria (preferiblemente en ramas técnicas, matemáticas o ingenierías).
Experiencia mínima de 3 años en posiciones similares.
Conocimientos sólidos de análisis de datos y analítica empresarial.
Manejo de SQL para consulta, extracción y tratamiento de datos.
Manejo de Python para análisis, tratamiento y automatización de procesos.
Experiencia con herramientas de visualización y reporting, especialmente Microsoft Power BI.
Conocimientos de estadística aplicada al análisis de datos.
Conocimientos de minería de datos.
Familiaridad con entornos y conceptos de Big Data.
Experiencia o familiaridad con Salesforce como herramienta de CRM.
Manejo habitual de herramientas de Microsoft Office, especialmente Excel y PowerPoint.
Capacidad para interpretar información compleja y traducirla en conclusiones claras para negocio.
Capacidad para trabajar con múltiples fuentes de información y estructurarlas de forma útil y accionable.


Se Ofrece


Fecha de incorporación: inmediata.
Contrato indefinido.
Jornada completa.
Ubicación: Campus Villafranca (Madrid).


En SEK Education Group utilizamos herramientas tecnológicas con funcionalidades de inteligencia artificial como apoyo en la gestión de nuestros procesos de selección. Estas herramientas nos ayudan con tareas como la gestión de comunicaciones y la organización de candidaturas. Todas las decisiones sobre tu candidatura las toma siempre una persona de nuestro equipo. Más información en nuestra política de privacidad: http://rgpd.sek.es/politica-privacidad

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Before applying: please note that, to be eligible for this position, you must be currently enrolled at a university in Spain, remain enrolled until the end of the internship, and be eligible to sign an internship agreement with your university. You must also be available to start the internship in October 2026 and continue participating in the program for a minimum of 5 months and a maximum of 12 months.

The Customer Support (CS) organization delivers services for all PPS customers across EMEA through a network of outsourced partners.

We are looking for a Data Analyst Intern to join the Delivery Cost & Operations team within the Service Delivery Support and Business Management organization.

This is an internship position designed as a versatile analyst profile, supporting the team across multiple priorities including automation (including AI), operational performance, and financial analysis.

You will be part of an international team, working in a dynamic environment and contributing directly to improving efficiency, cost management, and decision-making across EMEA markets.

Responsibilities


Design and maintain data analysis frameworks, models, and dashboards (Power BI, Excel) to support decision-making across EMEA Operations.
Develop and scale data automation solutions and pipelines, transforming manual processes into efficient workflows using SQL, Python, and AI-driven tools.
Manage and optimize data structures and datasets, ensuring high data quality, consistency, and readiness for advanced analytics.
Analyze operational and financial performance (KPIs, costs, efficiency metrics) and translate data into actionable insights and recommendations.
Enhance reporting and scorecards, enabling automated performance tracking and supporting business reviews.
Contribute to process improvement and transformation initiatives, driving automation, standardization, and simplification across teams.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to embed data-driven solutions into operational and financial processes.


Requirements


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Strong foundation in data engineering and analytics, including data modeling techniques and analytical methods.
Hands-on experience with SQL, Python, or R for data manipulation, analysis, or automation.
Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI) and ability to build dashboards and communicate insights.
Basic understanding of data pipelines and big data concepts is a plus.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, PowerPoint), with strong Excel skills as an advantage.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to work with complex datasets.
Fluent English (written and spoken) in an international environment.
Curious, proactive, and collaborative mindset, with enthusiasm for automation and data-driven decision making.


Experience Our Benefits


Paid internship within a global, technology driven company.
Join an international work environment, with opportunities for learning, professional development, and networking across teams.
Flexible working schedule, with options for part-time agreements (4 hours/day) or full-time (8 hours/day).  The length of the internship ranges from 4 to 12 months.
Access to our intern community, including career development workshops, learning sessions, and social activities that help you grow professionally and connect with other interns.
Mentor assigned.
Opportunity to join the HP Graduate Program following the placement.
Exclusive HP products offers and discounts for employees, friends and family.
Lunch at reduced prices at our canteen.
Access to our on‑site sports center, including:
Gym facilities
Squash, tennis, and basketball courts
25+ organized sports and wellness activities, such as HIIT training, squash, basketball, and yoga
Participation in the NextGen Employee Network, offering regular events and opportunities to connect with other early‑career professionals.
On‑site medical services, including access to a doctor, nutrition support, physiotherapy, and general healthcare.
Free printing access and hands‑on workshops using the latest HP technologies — from large‑format prints to 3D‑printed models.
Easy commuting: Free parking available or excellent access via nearby train services.
Women Network initiatives: Networking events, STEM promotion, business acumen talks, work–life balance sessions, and future‑skills development.

Title: Data Analyst - Risk & Internal Audit F/M
Description:
Why Join Us?




Alliance Automotive Group (AAG) is a leading automotive parts distributor serving independent aftermarket clients across Europe, operating in France, the UK, Ireland, Germany, Spain, Portugal, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Poland.

As part of Genuine Parts Company (GPC), a global leader in automotive and industrial parts distribution, you will join a dynamic, international organization committed to growth, innovation, and operational excellence.

You will be part of AAG’s Internal Audit & Risk Management Department, working at the intersection of business performance, risk management, and advanced analytics across multiple European markets.




Your Impact




As a Data Analyst - Risk & Audit (M/F), you will transform data into high-impact insights and scalable data products that:

Strengthen fraud prevention and internal controls
Enable continuous audit and risk monitoring
Improve data quality and governance at a European level
Accelerate data-driven decision-making and risk identification




Your Responsibilities




Business & Risk Analytics

Extract, clean, and analyze data from multiple systems.
Detect anomalies, trends, and potential fraud indicators and prevention.
Translate business challenges into actionable analytics solutions.

BI Development & Data Visualization

Design, develop, and optimize Power BI dashboards & manage monthly BI releases and enhancements.
Develop complex queries using SQL and Python.

Automation & Continuous Monitoring

Design automated audit and control monitoring tools.
Implement data quality checks and automated validation rules.
Enable continuous risk assessment through advanced analytics.
Leverage AI tools and automation technologies to improve audit and operational efficiency.

Data Quality & Governance

Assess data quality and identify and lead remediation actions.
Collaborate with data governance experts to ensure integrity and reliability.

Our Technology Stack

Methodologies: Agile, Design Thinking
Cloud Platform: Microsoft Fabric
Visualization tools: PowerBI
Data languages & tools: SQL, Python




Profile




Graduate from an engineering school or university (Master’s degree) with a specialization in Information Systems, Data, or Data Science
Proven 3 to 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst, with at least one expertise in data processing and data visualization tools
Curious, methodical, with a solid technology mindset and an interest in exploratory data analysis
Eager to contribute to innovative projects, and interested in new technologies
Experience working with large-scale reporting environments
Fluent in English, another language is a plus

Title: Data Analyst - Madrid
Description:
¿Quieres formar parte del equipo de Serveo?




Si quieres que cada día sea diferente, enfrentándote a nuevos desafíos y formando parte de un equipo que te permita desarrollar todas tus capacidades, ¡sigue leyendo!




En Serveo somos un equipo multidisciplinar, diverso e inclusivo, formado por más de 48.000 profesionales. Somos un referente en servicios deindustria, salud, corporate, government e infraestructuras ofreciendo soluciones integrales, innovadoras y sostenibles.




Sonreímos al cambio y afrontamos juntos cada nuevo reto, impulsando la excelencia de nuestros servicios y fomentando el crecimiento de nuestros equipos en un extraordinario ambiente de trabajo.




¡Estamos orgullosos/as de ser reconocidos como una de las 100 mejores empresas para trabajar en España por Actualidad Económica (El Mundo) y por Forbes y de estar en el Top 5 del Happy Trainees en 2025!







¿En qué consiste el puesto?




Buscamos un Data Analyst en Madrid, cuya misión será transformar los datos en conocimiento accionable para apoyar la toma de decisiones en la organización, analizando, interpretando y visualizando información procedente de múltiples fuentes para identificar tendencias, patrones y oportunidades de mejora.




Funciones a desarrollar:




Analizar y explorar datos, identificando tendencias, patrones y oportunidades que aporten valor al negocio.
Diseñar, desarrollar y optimizar cuadros de mando e informes, utilizando herramientas de visualización (principalmente Power BI) para facilitar la toma de decisiones.
Traducir necesidades de negocio en requerimientos analíticos, colaborando con las distintas áreas para definir métricas e indicadores clave (KPIs).
Garantizar la calidad y coherencia de los análisis, validando la integridad de los datos tanto en origen como en el Data Warehouse y asegurando la correcta interpretación de los resultados.
Colaborar con otros perfiles de datos, asegurando la disponibilidad, calidad y adecuación de los datos necesarios para el análisis.
Documentar métricas, indicadores y definiciones, manteniendo un catálogo claro y consistente que facilite el autoservicio analítico.
Impulsar la cultura del dato y el autoservicio, apoyando a los equipos de negocio en el uso autónomo de las herramientas analíticas.
Apoyar la toma de decisiones basada en datos, presentando conclusiones y recomendaciones de forma clara a perfiles técnicos y de negocio.
Aplicar estándares de gobierno, calidad y seguridad del dato, asegurando el cumplimiento de las políticas corporativas en el tratamiento de la información.
Analizar y comprender la información de negocio de forma integral, identificando oportunidades de mejora y proponiendo nuevos indicadores, análisis y cuadros de mando que aporten valor a la organización.
Participar en la definición e implantación de controles y mecanismos de calidad del dato, monitorizando la información y estableciendo procesos de alerta y comunicación con los Data Owners ante la detección de incidencias o anomalías.
Colaborar en el diseño y evolución de datasets analíticos y modelos de datos orientados al reporting y al autoservicio, participando en procesos de integración y transformación de datos.







¿Qué buscamos?




Grado en ingeniería informática, matemáticas, estadística, ADE o similar
Experiencia sólida en análisis de datos y dominio de SQL para la consulta, explotación y optimización de datos.
Dominio de herramientas de visualización (principalmente Power BI).
Conocimiento de arquitecturas de datos en plataformas cloud, especialmente Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Knowledge Catalog, etc.).
Conocimiento de Python para análisis, tratamiento y automatización de datos.
Inglés (Avanzado)
Entre 3 y 5 años de experiencia en análisis de datos, Business Intelligence o analítica de datos en entornos corporativos con las tecnologías mencionadas.
Perfil con capacidad analítica, comunicación, orientación a resultados, mejora continua, atención al detalle, autonomía y proactividad, trabajo en equipo







¿Qué te ofrecemos?




Contrato indefinido: te ofrecemos un proyecto a largo plazo en una empresa líder en servicios de infraestructuras.




Flexibilidad para facilitar la conciliación. Serveo ha sido galardonada con el Certificado EFR de la Fundación Más Familia, por su implicación en la conciliación personal, familiar y laboral. En este puesto tendrás:
Flexibilidad horaria de entrada y salida
Jornada continua en verano y festividades
Política corporativa de teletrabajo




Paquete retributivo competitivo.
Retribución total altamente competitiva en el sector, compuesta por una retribución fija más una retribución variable
Acceso a una competitiva 'Bolsa Corporativa de Beneficios' anual que podrás destinar con toda flexibilidad a diferentes productos (tarjeta comida, seguro de salud, tarjeta transporte público, cheque guardería, formación o plan de ahorro) según tus intereses, lo que te permitirá optimizar la fiscalidad de tu retribución y disfrutar de importantes descuentos a los que tenemos acceso como gran empresa.




Desarrollo y proyección profesional: a través de la participación en proyectos transversales, formación y movilidad interna.







Excepcional ambiente de trabajo, en el que te sentirás como ‘en familia’ desde el primer día. En Serveo impulsamos una forma de trabajar que deja de lado las jerarquías y empodera a cada persona para ser líder en su día a día.







Despierta tu talento. Impulsa tu futuro. Supérate con nosotros.




¡Conócenos más entrando en: Personas | Serveo - Impulsores de un mejor futuro!

Title: Analista de datos
Description:
🧩 Oferta de Empleo: Desarrollador/a Python – Automatización de Procesos




Modalidad: Híbrido - Boadilla del Monte - los 2 primeros meses (3 días presencial y 2 remoto), luego remoto 100%

Tipo de contrato: Indefinido

Jornada: Tiempo completo (horario de 09:00-18:00)

Salario: A valorar por perfil




🌐 Sobre el puesto

Desde SOTEC Consulting – Grupo Astek buscamos un/a Desarrollador/a Python con experiencia en automatización de procesos para incorporarse a un proyecto de transformación y optimización de procesos operativos en el sector bancario.

Buscamos un perfil con al menos 2 años de experiencia desarrollando automatizaciones mediante Python, capaz de identificar oportunidades de mejora, optimizar flujos de trabajo y colaborar con diferentes áreas de negocio para impulsar la eficiencia operativa mediante soluciones tecnológicas.




🪛 Funciones

• Desarrollo e implementación de automatizaciones de procesos y tareas utilizando Python.

• Análisis, optimización y reingeniería de procesos operativos.

• Identificación de oportunidades de mejora y eficiencia en los flujos de trabajo.

• Elaboración y mantenimiento de documentación técnica y funcional.

• Colaboración con equipos de negocio para automatizar procesos y mejorar la productividad.

• Análisis y tratamiento de datos para apoyar la toma de decisiones.




🛠️ Requisitos obligatorios

• Al menos 2 años de experiencia en automatización y optimización de procesos.

• Experiencia desarrollando automatizaciones y scripts con Python.

• Conocimientos de SQL para consultas y gestión de bases de datos.

• Experiencia en análisis de datos y tratamiento de información.

• Conocimientos de reingeniería de procesos y mejora continua.

• Titulación universitaria (Grado, Licenciatura o equivalente).

• Nivel de inglés alto.




📃 Requisitos valorables

• Experiencia con herramientas de análisis de datos y reporting.

• Capacidad para trabajar en entornos multidisciplinares y colaborar con áreas de negocio.

• Habilidades de documentación técnica y funcional.




🎯 Lo que ofrecemos

• Participación en proyectos innovadores y de alto impacto.

• Cultura colaborativa con equipos multidisciplinarios.

• Planes de desarrollo profesional y formación continua.

• Beneficios adicionales.




¿Te interesa desarrollar soluciones que optimicen procesos y generen un impacto real en el negocio? ¡Queremos conocerte!




🌍 ¿Por qué aplicar con SOTEC / ASTEK?

Formar parte de SOTEC Consulting significa integrarte en una compañía con más de 30 años de experiencia en el sector IT, respaldada por la solidez del Grupo ASTEK.

Gracias a esta integración, tendrás acceso a proyectos nacionales e internacionales de gran escala, colaborando con equipos multidisciplinares y participando en iniciativas que impulsan la innovación tecnológica.




SOTEC es una compañía comprometida con la igualdad de oportunidades, sin tener en cuenta raza, sexo, religión, edad, orientación sexual, estado civil, discapacidad, nacionalidad o identidad de género. Nos esforzamos cada día por construir procesos de selección inclusivos y libres de sesgos.




📈 Da el siguiente paso en tu carrera y forma parte de proyectos que están transformando el entorno digital.




📩 ¡Envíanos tu candidatura!
