Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:

• DECSKILL TALENT: through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;

• DECSKILL BOOST: through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;

• DECSKILL CONNECT: through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a DATA ANALYST.




Responsabilities:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team. Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals. It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




Requierments:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV in English to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst".




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

Hybrid working model;
Flexible working hours;
Health & Life Insurance;
Meal allowance paid in Meal Card;
Additional Days off: extra vacation day, employee’s birthday, volunteering day;
Opportunity to grow professionally inside the Company;
Possibility to participate in multicultural projects;
Several internal activities aiming to promote our team´s wellbeing.




📩 Apply now! Send your CV to Recruitment.SBS.Portugal@sodexo.com with the job title in the subject line. Explore all our career opportunities here: Sodexo Careers.

Learn more about Sodexo: YouTube - Sodexo.




Diversity & Inclusion Statement

Sodexo is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit.




🔒 Data Privacy:

We respect your privacy. Your personal data will be used exclusively for recruitment purposes, processed under GDPR regulations, and treated confidentially. For further details, contact privacy.sbs@sodexo.com.

HTML:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!



We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst;

Seniority: Mid-Level / Senior (+3 years);

Type of position: Hybrid (Porto);


The amazing you, will have:

• Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields;

• Professional experience in Data Analysis;

• Strong experience with Python (Pandas);

• Experience with SQL and data manipulation;

• Professional experience with Tableau and/or Power BI;

• Experience with Power Query and DAX;

• Knowledge of Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto or Airflow;

• Experience creating dashboards, reports and data visualizations;

• Ability to analyse, synthesize and simplify complex information;

• Strong problem-solving and analytical skills;

• Experience gathering business requirements and translating them into technical solutions;

• Ability to communicate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders;

• Fluent level of English;


Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Innovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Description:

At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Engineer!




Responsibilities:

Perform analysis of internal and external data to identify business growth opportunities and support client strategy initiatives.
Ensure accurate tracking and delivery of KPIs aligned with client development goals and business roadmap.
Support global teams (London, New York, Hong Kong) by providing high-quality data insights, tools, and analysis to improve sales effectiveness and decision-making.
Produce high-value reports, dashboards, and documentation for regional and global client reviews.
Leverage multiple data sources to generate actionable insights on business performance, client profitability, and wallet share growth.
Promote and contribute to the automation of reporting and client intelligence deliverables in collaboration with business and IT teams.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across multiple data pipelines, dashboards, and reporting systems.
Support the development and improvement of client data processes, analytics frameworks, and reporting standards.




Requirements:

Technical Requirements

Strong experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or data engineering-related roles.
Solid knowledge of:
Data visualization and BI tools such as:
Tableau
Power BI
Strong programming skills in Python, particularly using Pandas for data analysis.
Strong knowledge of SQL and experience working with ETL processes.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing.
Experience working with large, complex datasets and multi-source data environments.
Experience in report automation and data pipeline optimization is highly valued.

Soft Skills

Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to translate data into clear business insights.
Adaptability and ability to work in fast-paced, global environments.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and accuracy.
Results-driven mindset with strong delivery focus.
Problem-solving skills with the ability to design and improve analytical processes.
Proactive attitude and ability to work effectively within a team.
Strong collaboration skills across business and technical stakeholders.

Language Requirements

English: Mastery level (mandatory).




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us:

Long-term projects with national and international context (if applicable)
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com, with the reference “CA/DataAnalyst”.




Thank you! :)

Title: Analista de Dados (M/F)
HTML:
A GreenVenture é uma empresa privada de investimento e gestão de ativos especializada na identificação, aquisição, desenvolvimento e implementação de projetos de energia renovável.


Acreditamos no poder da ação coletiva e da inovação para criar um futuro sustentável. Procuramos reforçar a nossa estrutura com um Analista de dados (m/f).


Missão:

Para suportar o crescimento das nossas áreas de produção e comercialização de energia, procuramos profissional para desenvolvimento de modelos matemáticos aplicados a mercados de eletricidade;


Responsabilidades:

  • Desenvolvimento e manutenção de modelos de previsão multi-variável e multi-horizonte (produção solar e eólica, consumo, preço, desvios);
  • Desenvolver dashboards de monitorização de desempenho e relatórios analíticos (KPIs de precisão, eficiência e retorno);
  • Implementar rotinas de data cleaning, atualização automática de datasets e version control de modelos;
  • Sistemas de decisão em tempo real para mercados grossistas e mercados de serviços de sistema;
  • Colaborar com a equipa técnica e de gestão na interpretação dos resultados preditivos e na sua integração nas decisões de trading;
  • Validação, monitorização e backtesting de modelos em ambiente operacional;
  • Colaboração com a equipa de operação na definição de requisitos e avaliação de resultados.


Perfil:

  • Formação superior em Matemática (pura ou aplicada), Física ou Engenharia Física. Áreas afins com sólida base quantitativa (Estatística, Data Science) poderão ser consideradas;
  • Domínio sólido de Python (numpy, pandas); familiaridade com bibliotecas de ML e otimização (scikit-learn, PyTorch, Pyomo, OR-Tools) constitui uma mais-valia;
  • Conforto com séries temporais, modelação probabilística, otimização sob restrições e teoria da decisão;
  • Capacidade de trabalho com dados incompletos, multi-fonte e em diferentes granularidades temporais;
  • Pensamento crítico e rigor analítico.


Oferta:

  • Integração numa empresa sólida e em crescimento;
  • Equipa com estrutura profissional e dinâmica;
  • Remuneração em função da experiência demonstrada.

Title: Data analyst & dashboard designer
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side


Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau
Use Dataiku and PostgreSQL for advanced data processing and statistical analysis
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover hidden correlations and generate additional insights
Continuously improve data models to enhance accuracy, usability and performance


Run Side


Ensure data accuracy, consistency and availability across dashboards
Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes


This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
ETL tools and data modeling best practices
Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
Basic Python skills
Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)


Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills


English: C1


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Excellent communication skills in English (French is a plus)
Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybjrid (50%)

Range: €39,000–€52,000 (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data / Cybersecurity / IT Risk

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data Analyst

This is your mission

Are you passionate about transforming data into actionable insights? Do you enjoy working closely with business stakeholders and building analytical solutions that drive decision-making? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst, you will work at the intersection of business and technology, helping transform complex data into meaningful insights, dashboards, reports, and automation solutions. You will collaborate directly with business stakeholders to understand their challenges, identify opportunities, and deliver data-driven solutions that support strategic decision-making.

Build Side


Gather and analyze business requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Extract, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple data sources.
Design and develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools.
Build automated reporting solutions to improve business efficiency and decision-making.
Develop data analysis workflows using Python and SQL.
Contribute to the industrialization of data feeds, ETL processes, and data quality initiatives.
Create data models and analytical frameworks to generate business insights.
Partner with technical teams to deliver scalable and sustainable analytics solutions.


Run Side


Maintain and enhance existing dashboards, reports, and analytical tools.
Monitor data quality and ensure the reliability of reporting solutions.
Support business users by providing analysis and recommendations based on data findings.
Present analytical results and insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and improve operational efficiency.
Troubleshoot reporting and data-related issues.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams in an agile and fast-paced environment.
Continuously improve reporting standards, methodologies, and visualization practices.


This is your background

You have experience working with data analysis, reporting, and business intelligence solutions. You enjoy solving business problems through data and are comfortable interacting with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

You are curious, analytical, and motivated by transforming data into impactful business decisions

This Is Your Expertise


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Professional experience in Data Analysis or Business Intelligence roles.
Strong experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI.
Experience with data analysis using Python, particularly Pandas.
Good SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation.
Ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information.
Experience creating dashboards and presenting analytical findings to stakeholders.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, or Airflow is a plus.
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus.
Experience working in financial services, capital markets, or trading environments is considered an advantage.


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Ability to explain complex concepts in a clear and concise manner.
Business-oriented mindset with strong stakeholder management skills.
Curious and proactive approach to identifying improvement opportunities.
Passion for automation and process optimization.
Adaptable and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Enjoys tackling programming and analytical challenges.


Language Skills :


English: C1


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybrid (50%)

Salary: From 28K€ to 40K€ (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data Analytics / Business Intelligence / Data Visualization

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.

Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid

What Will You Do As Data Analyst


Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.


Key Qualifications

Must have:


Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.


Nice Have


Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.


Personal Skills


Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.


What ISS Offers You

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:


Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.


Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.

ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.

ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst
Description:
Want to know how a leading workplace experience and facility management company is powered by technology?

At ISS Tech Team, we create smarter workplace experiences through technology. Our Global Technology Solutions team builds digital solutions, from sensors and IoT devices to data, analytics and AI-driven capabilities, that support our ISS A/S operations across Cleaning, Workplace, Food and Technical Services.

With tech hubs in Copenhagen, Warsaw, and Porto, and teams collaborating across more than 30 countries, our ISS Tech team develops our digital solutions that have a real impact on both our customers and our people.

Location: Porto, Portugal

Work regime: Hybrid

What will you do as Data Analyst:


Provide day‑to‑day operational support for quarterly carbon data collection and input validation.
Maintain strong data‑quality control at the input and pre‑processing stage to ensure smooth downstream reporting by the country decarb managers.
Support the implementation of standardised validation checks, anomaly detection, and country follow‑ups.
Assist in preparing clean, validated datasets for further analysis and reporting.
Contribute to audit‑readiness by organising documentation and evidence.



Key qualifications:

Must have:


Master’s Degree in Data or Environmental Science
More than 3 years’ experience in data handling, environmental data, or similar analytical support roles.
Strong skills in Excel, Power BI (basic visuals, data prep), and SQL (simple queries, data extraction).
Experience with data cleaning, validation, and structured data workflows.
English proficiency, both written and spoken.



Nice have:


Familiarity with emissions data or GHG concepts is an advantage but not required.
Comfortable in repetitive, detail-oriented validation work.



Personal skills:


Strong attention to detail and careful execution.
Strong communication and reliability under time pressure.



What ISS Offers You:

We focus on creating an environment where you can grow and perform at your best:


Permanent contract.
Private health insurance.
22 days of annual leave + 3 additional company days to recharge.
Flexible working hours and a hybrid model that supports balance and collaboration.
Continuous learning opportunities (technical and behavioural development) to support your growth.
Modern, well-equipped office in the heart of Porto.
Free parking for your convenience.
Access to ISS Tech Communities to connect, learn and share knowledge.
Free access to the company gym to support your wellbeing.
Snacks and drinks available at the office.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
At LUXCLUSIF, we are dedicated to redefining the luxury paradigm by offering premium pre-owned fashion to a global audience. As a pioneer in the global luxury resale market, we utilize advanced data-driven solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of high-end pre-owned items. With operations spread across the globe and strategic warehouses in key locations, we ensure a seamless supply of luxury goods. Our comprehensive services cover everything from item curation, quality control, operations, logistics to technology integrations and sales, delivering an unmatched resale experience for brands, retailers, and individual customers alike.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
As a Senior Data Analyst, you will join our Product Analytics team to champion data best practices and strengthen our technical foundations. You will deliver impactful insights regarding our tech products while playing a pivotal role in evolving our self-service data environment. By partnering closely with our Data Engineering team and power users across verticals, you will centralize business logic, optimize our data models, and ensure our analytics products remain performant, scalable, and governed.

What You'll Do


Centralize Business Logic: Identify decentralized logic (such as complex cost calculations currently living inside Power BI semantic models) and push it upstream into centralized, well-documented data models
Empower Self-Service: Provide our vertical power users with a clean, highly performant, and governed data foundation so they can confidently build the reports they need independently
Champion Data Governance: Drive data governance efforts across our growing self-service ecosystem, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the company
Mentor & Elevate: Act as technical anchor for our team, mentoring on SQL optimization, dimensional modeling, and BI best practices
Deliver Product Analytics: Maintain and evolve core company reports while providing deep-dive analytics on our tech products to support strategic business decisions





Who You Are


Experienced Professional: You have 5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Analytics Engineering, or Business Intelligence, with a strong track record of improving data infrastructure and performance
Tech Savvy: You possess expert-level SQL skills and extensive experience with Power BI (including DAX optimization and semantic models) and cloud data platforms like Azure (particularly Databricks). Ideally, you also bring hands-on experience with dbt and Google BigQuery as we plan to evolve our data stack
Architectural Thinker: You can easily identify bottlenecks in data pipelines or decentralized logic, and you take the initiative to design and implement efficient, scalable solutions
Collaborative Mentor: You are a team player who enjoys collaborating closely with data team members and stakeholders
Effective Communicator: You excel at translating complex technical concepts and governance standards to non-technical stakeholders and power users





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities,
Flexible benefits program





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT


FARFETCH is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.





SCAM DISCLAIMER


It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent FARFETCH in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that FARFETCH does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, FARFETCH will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process





We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Steward
HTML:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!


A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes — e é exatamente isso que nos move.

Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 12 anos de expertise no mercado e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido, em projetos em mais de 20 países.

Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso. Por isso, defendemos a valorização, o reconhecimento e o crescimento contínuo dos nossos profissionais.

Não queremos ser apenas mais uma linha no teu CV. Queremos que vivas connosco uma verdadeira Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formação e certificações que comprovam a tua progressão real, promovemos um saudável work-life balance e oferecemos benefícios com impacto direto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.

#welcometoyourfuture


O que procuramos:

  • +3 anos de experiência em Data Management, Business Analysis ou funções similares;
  • Bons conhecimentos de Data Governance, incluindo qualidade de dados e gestão de metadados;
  • Familiaridade com ferramentas de Data Governance (Data Catalogs, Data Quality Observability);
  • Capacidade de comunicação e colaboração entre equipas técnicas e de negócio;
  • Pensamento crítico, atenção ao detalhe e foco na resolução de problemas;
  • Conhecimentos em Python, JavaScript ou linguagens similares para scripting;
  • Inglês fluente.


Ser Affinity


· #Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;

· #Ambição – Querer fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;

· #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;

· #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e de te tornares uma referência no mercado tecnológico;

· #Assertividade – Comunicação transparente e honesta, sempre com feedback construtivo.

A tua #LifetimeExperience

Title: Business Data Analyst (Maintenance)
Description:
Imagine your career taking you to the depths of innovation and the heights of impact. Our people enable continuous progress. Their commitment, collective expertise, and unique capabilities are the engine room behind SBM Offshore’s True. Blue. Transition. - shaping the future of energy, and beyond.

About Us:

SBM Offshore is a global leader in deepwater ocean infrastructure, delivering floating production solutions across the full asset lifecycle—from design and construction to installation and operation. Supported by a global team of more than 8,000 professionals, the Company operates a long-term, asset-backed business model that delivers high-availability assets and predictable cash flows. SBM Offshore combines engineering expertise, operational reliability, and selective innovation to support safe, efficient, and lower-carbon energy production, while extending its capabilities into new opportunities across the blue economy.

Purpose



As a Business Data Analyst, you analyze and interpret complex data to provide actionable insights that support business decision-making and strategy development. Work closely with stakeholders to identify data needs, create reports, and develop data models that drive business performance and optimize operational efficiency. Ensure data accuracy and contribute to the continuous improvement of data processes and tools.



Responsibilities



Analyze and interpret complex data sets to provide actionable insights that support business decision-making and strategy development.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify business needs and data requirements, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and objectives.
Develop and maintain data models, dashboards, and reports, providing clear and concise visualizations that communicate key findings to stakeholders.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity by performing data validation, cleansing, and quality checks, maintaining high standards of data governance.
Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into analytical tools, ensuring data is prepared and structured for analysis.
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies within data sets, providing insights that drive business process improvements and operational efficiency.
Support the development and implementation of data-driven strategies, working closely with business units to optimize performance and achieve key objectives.
Present findings and recommendations to senior management and stakeholders, translating complex data insights into understandable and actionable business terms.
Stay current with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analytics, integrating new tools and methodologies to enhance analytical capabilities.
Provide training and support to team members and other departments on data analysis techniques and the use of analytical tools, fostering a data-driven culture within the organization.



Education



Bachelor's degree in data science, business analytics, computer science, statistics, or a related field is required. Certifications such as Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP), Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate, or Certified Analytics Professional (CAP) are highly recommended. Proficiency in data analysis, statistical tools, and business intelligence (BI) platforms is essential. A master’s degree in a related field is preferred but not mandatory.



Experience



Minimum of 5 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field, with demonstrated expertise in analyzing business data to support decision-making processes.
Proven experience in collecting, cleaning, and analyzing large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that drive business performance.
Strong technical background with hands-on experience in using data analysis tools and languages such as SQL, Python, R, or Excel, and proficiency in business intelligence platforms like Power BI, Tableau, or Qlik.
Demonstrated ability to develop and implement data models, reports, and dashboards that provide actionable insights to stakeholders across the organization.
Experience in working closely with business units to understand their data needs, translating business requirements into data-driven solutions that support strategic goals.
Proficiency in data visualization techniques, with the ability to present complex data in a clear and compelling manner to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Knowledge of data governance, data quality management, and best practices for ensuring data integrity and accuracy across systems.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to identify data-related issues and recommend effective solutions to improve business processes.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams, including IT, finance, and operations, to support data-driven initiatives.
Relevant certifications such as Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP), Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate, or Certified Analytics Professional (CAP) are preferred.



Functional Competencies

Compliance

Governance, Risk and Control

Analytics and reporting

IT Tools and application

Cost and budget control

Business Partnering

Digital savvy

Business Acumen

Contract Management

Management of change application

GENERAL INFORMATION

SBM Offshore N.V. is a listed holding company that is headquartered in Amsterdam. It holds direct and indirect interests in other companies that collectively with SBM Offshore N.V. form the SBM Offshore Group (“the Company”). For further information, please visit our website at www.sbmoffshore.com. The companies in which SBM Offshore N.V., directly and indirectly, owns investments are separate entities.

SBM Offshore is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. SBM Offshore does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

Title: Data Governance Analyst
Description:
🚀 We’re hiring | Data Governance Analyst 🚀




At Empa‑T, we believe that technology grows best when people are at the center.




We are looking for a Data Governance Analyst to join an international project, where you will play a key role in ensuring data quality, consistency, and governance across the organization.




You will work closely with business and technical teams, contributing to data-driven decision making in a dynamic and collaborative environment.




Key Responsibilities

Support the implementation and evolution of Data Governance practices
Manage and maintain data catalogs and metadata
Monitor and improve data quality processes and controls
Collaborate with business and IT teams to define data standards and rules
Develop scripts to automate data-related processes
Ensure data consistency, lineage, and traceability
Identify opportunities for continuous improvement in data management




Required Skills & Experience

+2 years of experience in Data Management or Data Analysis
Solid understanding of Data Governance principles
Experience or exposure to Data Governance tools, including: Data Catalogs, Data Quality / Monitoring solutions
Knowledge of scripting languages (e.g., SQL, Python, or similar)
Strong communication skills and ability to work with cross-functional teams
Fluency in English (mandatory)




📍 Location

Porto – Hybrid (2x/office)




Why Empa‑T?

At Empa‑T, we value empathy, inclusion, integrity, and commitment.

We combine strong technical expertise with a human‑centric culture, creating an environment where people can grow, collaborate, and make a real impact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY




Join a data intelligence team focused on transforming procurement-related information into clear, reliable and actionable Power BI visualisations. You will support a procurement digitalisation context, helping move Excel-based reporting into more structured SQL-driven data models and visual analytics.




WHAT YOU WILL DO




- Build and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards for business data visualisation.

- Query, prepare and validate data using SQL.

- Transform Excel-based reporting processes into scalable Power BI visual solutions.

- Work with a senior team member while taking ownership of daily analytical delivery.

- Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand procurement processes and translate them into reporting requirements.

- Ensure data accuracy, consistency and usability across dashboards and reporting outputs.

- Support continuous improvement in data visualisation practices and reporting efficiency.




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR




- 3 to 5 years of professional experience in data analysis, business intelligence or analytics.

- Daily hands-on experience with SQL querying and Power BI development.

- Strong autonomy in technical delivery, with the ability to work with guidance from senior professionals.

- Advanced Power BI capability, including data modelling and dashboard design.

- Professional working proficiency in Portuguese and Spanish.

- Ability to understand business processes and learn domain-specific knowledge quickly.

- Responsible, collaborative and solution-oriented approach to delivery.




TECHNICAL SKILLS




- Power BI

- SQL

- Data querying

- Data modelling

- Data visualisation

Title: Business Insights Analyst
Description:
This is a remote role.

About Us

Reachdesk is the first truly end-to-end global SaaS gifting and swag platform, helping B2B companies create meaningful connections with prospects, customers, and employees: while driving measurable ROI.

We believe gifting is more than just a transaction: it’s about building genuine human connections that fuel business growth. Whether you’re engaging prospects, celebrating customers, or recognizing employees, our platform makes it easy to deliver personalized gifts, branded swag, and unforgettable event & brand experiences at scale through automated sourcing, storage, and global delivery.

Trusted by many of the most recognized names in technology and SaaS, Reachdesk integrates effortlessly with your marketing, sales, and HRIS tech stack and is powered by a world-class team with hubs in New York, London, and Lisbon.

We’re growing, and we’re currently looking for a Business Insights Analyst to join our Business Systems & Operations team.

The Role

This is a role for someone energised by the connections between how the business sells, how it serves customers, how it runs operationally and makes money, and who wants to use AI to make those connections faster and clearer than ever.

Every function at Reachdesk sees a different slice of the company. You'll be the person who sits across all of them, using available data and reporting outputs from relevant systems, helping leaders apply consistent commercial and operational definitions when interpreting business performance, and helping leaders see the picture none of them could have produced alone.

Your Responsibilities


Partner with leaders across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Finance and Operations to understand the decisions they're each trying to make.
Work with available data from commercial, finance and operational systems to produce insights that connect different views of the business.
Use AI to compress synthesis from days into hours, and to keep our framing of key metrics consistent across functions.
Tell the story behind the numbers in a way that lands with whoever's in the room, commercial, financial or operational.
Create lightweight summaries and repeatable insight outputs so teams can keep answering their own follow-on questions.
Experiment with AI-assisted insight workflows, prompt patterns, evals and lightweight automations, and share what works across the team.


About You


2–4 years in an analyst, insights, strategy or consulting role with exposure to more than one function.
Fluency in at least two of: commercial (revenue, retention, GTM), financial (P&L, unit economics, FP&A), operational (throughput, fulfilment, cycle time).
A genuine AI-native working style; you already use LLMs as a core part of how you think, and you're excited to keep pushing.
Strong written and verbal storytelling; you can adapt your framing for a CRO, a CFO or a Head of Operations without missing a beat.
Comfort working with spreadsheets and reporting tools. Working knowledge of SQL is useful but not essential.


Preferred Expertise


B2B SaaS background.
Familiarity with NetSuite, Salesforce, or the corporate gifting and direct mail space.
Light experience with experiment design, attribution, pricing or operational throughput modeling.


Why Join Us


A team that genuinely treats AI as a leverage tool, not a buzzword.
The closest thing in the company to a true cross-functional vantage point; you'll see how every part of Reachdesk fits together.
You’ll work alongside a small, senior Business Systems & Operations team focused on strategic, cross-functional work.


The Benefits


Competitive salary with generous stock options
Flexible PTO that increases the longer you’re with us, plus extra flexibility for life’s unexpected moments
A genuinely collaborative culture with innovative, fun, and ambitious people


Privacy Policy: We process personal information submitted as part of your application in accordance with the Reachdesk Candidate Privacy Notice. Please review Reachdesk’s Privacy Policy here: www.reachdesk.com/candidate-privacy-policy

Title: Digital Transformation Analyst
Description:
M/F

Location: Porto, Portugal




ABOUT US

PARFOIS is a fast-growing women’s accessories brand based in Portugal, present in over 70 countries with more than 1,000 stores worldwide.




We operate in a dynamic and evolving environment, where data, innovation, and efficiency are key to driving business performance. We are looking for analytical and driven professionals who are eager to contribute to our digital transformation journey.




THE ROLE

As Digital Transformation Analyst (Controlling), you will play a key role in improving the accessibility, quality, and structure of business data, supporting the Buying Controlling team in data-driven decision-making.




You will contribute to the development of data solutions, reporting tools, and dashboards, enabling greater autonomy and efficiency across teams.




This role is ideal for someone with a strong analytical mindset, technical expertise in data tools, and a genuine interest in transforming data into business impact.




RESPONSIBILITIES

Develop and optimize data queries, data models, and automated reports to support business needs
Improve accessibility, reliability, and quality of business information for decision-making
Design and implement data visualization tools and dashboards for business users
Enable greater autonomy of buying and business teams in accessing and analyzing performance data
Contribute to data governance initiatives, improving data structure, consistency, and processes
Support the digital transformation of the Commercial Controlling area through new tools and solutions
Reduce manual processes through automation and optimization of reporting workflows




PROFILE

Degree in Management, Data, Engineering, or a related field
Advanced knowledge of Excel and strong experience with data analysis
SQL knowledge (intermediate to advanced)
Experience with Power BI or similar data visualization tools
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to structure and interpret large volumes of data
Understanding of data models and business reporting
Detail-oriented, structured, and solution-driven mindset
Strong collaboration skills and ability to work with cross-functional teams
Proactive and pragmatic approach




Apply now!

Title: Distributed Cloud | Power BI Specialist
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description

We are seeking a sharp and insightful Power BI Specialist to drive our Business Intelligence initiatives. You will be instrumental in translating complex business requirements into clear data models and compelling visualizations, ensuring strategic decision-making across the organization.

Key Responsibilities:


Design and develop dynamic dashboards and reports using Power BI to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide actionable business insights.
Gather and translate business requirements into technical specifications for data modeling and reporting solutions.
Develop, optimize, and execute SQL queries to extract, transform, and prepare data for analysis.
Perform data validation and collaborate with stakeholders to ensure data integrity and quality.
Drive the adoption of self-service BI best practices across the business.



Qualifications


3+ years of proven experience as a Data Analyst or BI Developer.
Strong mandatory proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating data.
Expert hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI for data modeling (DAX) and visualization.
Proven ability to successfully elicit and document business requirements.
Language Requirement: Professional fluency (B2+) in French is highly valued and may be a mandatory requirement for allocation on specific projects with Francophone clients.



Additional Information

The Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Data & Analytics Engineer (PostgreSQL & Power BI)
HTML:
Retail Consult is recruiting!

The impact of technology in retail is a game changer affecting every retailer from small, local shops to retail giants. Working at Retail Consult means being at the center of this industry in transformation. As specialists in technology solutions for retail, we provide solutions strategy, implementation, deployment, training, and support services for clients across a range of retail segments including fashion, grocery, pharmacy, do it yourself, telecommunications, and electronics. Work, train and learn with a growing team with over 300 people based in Portugal, the US, Germany, Brazil, Mexico, Chile, and China, who are transforming the business of retailers across the globe.


We are currently seeking a Data & Analytics Engineer (PostgreSQL & Power BI) to join our growing team in Porto, Portugal.


What We Are Offering:

  • The opportunity to join a growing company with people passionate about their work in an international environment;
  • The opportunity to work with best-in-breed methodologies, tools, and techniques;
  • Training and Mentoring/Talent Development programs;
  • Full package of Salary, perks, and benefits (Health Insurance, mobile allowance, and others);
  • Career Evolution;
  • Flexible working hours;
  • Extra days off;
  • Wellbeing program;
  • Partnerships with local businesses.


What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Work closely with Finance, Sales, HR, and Project Management teams to understand business needs and translate them into scalable, reliable, and high-quality data solutions;
  • Design, define, and evolve Data Lake / Data Warehouse architectures, including data layers, schemas, partitioning strategies, and modelling standards;
  • Model, optimize, and maintain PostgreSQL databases for analytical and reporting workloads, ensuring performance, reliability, and scalability;
  • Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports, including DAX measures, Row Level Security (RLS), and performance optimization best practices;
  • Collaborate with internal application and platform teams to ensure consistent data integration and reliable end-to-end data flows across enterprise systems;
  • Design and manage ETL/ELT pipelines between source systems and the central data platform, ensuring robust, scalable, and maintainable data processing;
  • Ensure data quality, deduplication, and proper handling of Slowly Changing Dimensions (SCDs) across analytical environments;
  • Coordinate with integration teams to orchestrate and monitor batch and near real-time data flows;
  • Take ownership of data solutions end-to-end, from requirements gathering and design decisions to production delivery and continuous improvement;
  • Proactively identify opportunities to improve data quality, platform efficiency, and business insight generation.


What We Are Looking For:

  • Experience designing and building Data Lakes and/or Data Warehouses;
  • Strong ability to interpret functional and business requirements and translate them into scalable technical data models;
  • Strong SQL and PostgreSQL expertise, including data modelling, indexing, partitioning, and performance optimization;
  • Experience using Microsoft Power BI, including dashboard development, DAX and Row Level Security is a differentiator;
  • General understanding of cloud fundamentals, including compute, storage, networking, and identity/access management;
  • Strong communication skills, particularly when working with non-technical stakeholders;
  • Structured and analytical mindset with strong problem-solving skills;
  • Ability to work independently with a high level of ownership, while collaborating effectively in cross-functional teams;
  • High attention to detail and strong commitment to data quality, performance, and reliability;
  • Proactive, accountable, and results-oriented working style;
  • Proficiency in English is mandatory.


If you think your profile matches our requirements and you are looking for an experience that offers opportunity, growth, balance, and a feeling of value, apply to this job submitting your CV at: www.retail-consult.com/en/work-with-us/ or jobs@retail-consult.com.

Retail Consult – Transforming Retail with Passion

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About The Job

In today's digital landscape, data is the cornerstone of success. Sofico recognizes this truth and is assembling a brand-new team to help harness the potential of data within the Miles Enterprise Suite.

As a Data Engineer at Sofico, you will play a crucial role in unlocking the full potential of the data ecosystem within Miles Enterprise. Your mission is to extract actionable insights from the rich data resources, catering to both on-platform and off-platform reporting needs.

Key Responsibilities:


Efficient extraction of data from the specialized internal data models of Miles Enterprise
Transformation of raw data into formats optimized for reporting and analysis
Provision of robust data management capabilities, including data entry and adjustments, validation, reconciliation, versioning, enrichment, and mapping
Provision of a solid infrastructure for security, auditability, orchestration, scheduling, monitoring, client extensibility and customization
Development of outbound data consumption interfaces for seamless integration with customers' existing data warehouses (or lakes) and BI platforms
Creation of a reference data warehouse c.q. purpose-specific data marts within Miles Enterprise to extend on-platform reporting capabilities
Design and implementation of various data visualizations, including enterprise reports, self-service BI tools, dashboards, and KPI metrics



By becoming a part of Sofico's forward-thinking data engineering team, you will be at the forefront of revolutionizing the automotive finance industry. Collaborating with passionate team members, you will redefine the boundaries of data utilization, shaping the future of our industry through your contributions.

Having established a solid foundation, you will have the chance to venture into more advanced topics such as predictive modeling, data mining, real-time data streaming, or integration with LLMs. Your role will continuously evolve, presenting new opportunities for professional development and innovation.

Your Interests And Skills

Your analytical skills and critical mindset will be important when dealing with data model, pipeline, and architectural complexity.

Attention to detail is also crucial for ensuring data accuracy, quality, and consistency.

You will work as a scrum team with the product owner to deliver on the platform requirements.

To thrive in this role, you’ll need:


3-5 years’ experience in a relevant data engineering role
Experience with PostgresSQL, Python and Java
Excellent communication skills in English, with fluency in Dutch considered a plus
You are comfortable working in scrum mode, your potential to act as a Scrum Master is a plus



What we offer you

We Offer


A competitive salary paid 14 times a year
Full medical insurance covered by Sofico
daily meal allowance for every day worked
Flexibility with adjustable hours and work-from-home options
A sleek, modern office with excellent accessibility and amenities
Tailored six-month onboarding with your own job coach
Exciting team events: think after-work drinks, BBQs, and more

Title: Digital Solutions & Data Engineer
Description:
At Aptiv, we’re driving the future of mobility — safely, sustainably, and intelligently. We are looking for a highly motivated and versatile professional to join our IT and Automation team, focusing on the development of digital solutions, systems integration, and support for digital transformation in operations

Please submit your application in English

YOUR ROLE


Design and development of applications and workflows using Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, and Dataflows for SharePoint).
Configuration and maintenance of Linux servers, including essential services such as HTTP, SMB, PHP, FTP, and other web interface components.
Development and automation of ETL and ELT pipelines for data integration and transformation.
Programming in Python (preferred), PHP, HTML, and C#.
Management and optimization of relational databases (MySQL/MariaDB).
Support and execution of Data Analytics projects, enabling data-driven decision-making.
Participation in Machine Learning and AI initiatives (optional but valued).


YOUR BACKROUND


Bachelors/Masters Degree in Data Engineering or Analytics or equivalent
Proven experience in Data Engineering or Data Analytics.
Solid knowledge of Power Platform and SharePoint integration.
Solid experience in Linux environments.
Experience with data pipeline automation.
Proficiency in multiple programming languages.
Familiarity with relational database systems.
Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively.
Problem-solving mindset and proactive attitude.


WHY JOIN US?


You can grow at Aptiv. Aptiv provides an inclusive work environment where all individuals can grow and develop, regardless of gender, ethnicity or beliefs.
You can have an impact. Safety is a core Aptiv value; we want a safer world for us and our children, one with: Zero fatalities, Zero injuries, Zero accidents.
You have support. We ensure you have the resources and support you need to take care of your family and your physical and mental health with a competitive health insurance package.


YOUR BENEFITS AT APTIV:


Competitive compensation package
Learning, professional growth and development in a world recognized international environment
Access to internal & external training
Recognition for innovation and excellence.
Opportunities to give back to the community
Meaningful work that makes a difference in the world


Please note that to be considered for this position, candidates must be legally eligible to work in Portugal. This includes having:


A valid residence permit or working visa
A Portuguese Tax Identification Number (NIF)
A Portuguese Social Security Number (NISS)


APPLY TODAY, AND TOGETHER LET’S CHANGE TOMORROW!

Title: Data analyst & dashboard designer
Description:
Every moment counts. Especially those you live to the fullest. Welcome to Consort Group.

For over 30 years, Consort Group has been helping companies leverage their data and infrastructure. It is backed by two leaders, Consortis and Consortia, and places people and social responsibility at the heart of its values.

This is your future team

Established in 2021, Consort Portugal supports the strategic offshoring choices of our clients, particularly in Europe, and brings our expertise in infrastructure services to the Portuguese market. With a team of around 45 employees, our Service Center based in Porto is developing a dynamic nearshore business, offering the French and European markets an environment of excellence and high potential.

Data analyst & dashboard designer

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analytics, cybersecurity risk management and data visualization? Then this position is for you.

As a Data Analyst & Dashboard Designer, you will be responsible for leading end-to-end dashboard initiatives within a large-scale international cybersecurity environment, contributing to Enterprise Security activities and cyber risk governance.

Build Side


Understand business requirements and identify relevant KPIs related to cybersecurity risk management
Design and implement robust data models to support cyber risk dashboards
Collect, clean and transform large datasets using ETL tools
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards using Power BI and Tableau
Use Dataiku and PostgreSQL for advanced data processing and statistical analysis
Leverage machine learning algorithms to uncover hidden correlations and generate additional insights
Continuously improve data models to enhance accuracy, usability and performance


Run Side


Ensure data accuracy, consistency and availability across dashboards
Monitor data quality and proactively address inconsistencies
Present findings and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate with cybersecurity, IT risk and resilience teams across multiple locations
Manage multiple dashboard projects simultaneously with autonomy and strong time management
Contribute to the continuous improvement of dashboard production processes


This is your background

You have at least 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst in demanding, data-driven environments.

You enjoy transforming complex datasets into meaningful visual insights and are constantly looking for ways to innovate through data science and automation. You are looking for an environment where your ideas matter and where technology goes hand in hand with collaboration.

This Is Your Expertise


Power BI & Tableau (dashboard design and advanced DataViz)
Dataiku (data preparation, data science workflows)
PostgreSQL (data extraction, transformation and querying)
ETL tools and data modeling best practices
Statistical analysis and basic machine learning concepts
Basic Python skills
Microsoft collaboration tools (PowerPoint, SharePoint, Teams)


Certifications such as Tableau Certified Data Analyst or Dataiku Advanced Designer are a plus.

Languag Skills


English: C1


This is how you work as part of a team:


Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Excellent communication skills in English (French is a plus)
Ability to collaborate effectively with distributed cybersecurity teams
Autonomous, organized and capable of managing multiple priorities
Proactive in identifying issues and proposing concrete solutions


This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.


Attentive and human onboarding
A truly hands-on management style
Continuous training opportunities
Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, TR card, CSE, pension plan
A culture of feedback and meaningful projects


The recruitment process:


An initial phone call with our recruitment team
An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
A test or technical interview with one of our experts
A final meeting with your future manager or project manager
And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together


Good to know

Location: Lisbon or Porto

Contract: Permanent contract

Remote work: Hybjrid (50%)

Range: €39,000–€52,000 (depending on experience) gross annual salary

Job category: Data / Cybersecurity / IT Risk

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.
